message.content:
I want you to first come up with a plan of action to accomplish the following prompt: Develop a JusticeOps Offline Legal Services & Scheduling system for organizations that coordinate legal casework, appointments, and compliance tasks in air-gapped or low-connectivity environments. The Angular web UI provides distinct workspaces for Client, Lawyer, and Administrator roles, with role-based menus and in-context permission feedback when actions are restricted. Users sign in with locally verified username and password, with password rules requiring at least 12 characters including one number and one symbol, and an automatic lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes. Clients can place booking orders for consultations or case milestones, request reschedules, or cancel, while seeing clear policy messages such as “no-show after 10 minutes past the scheduled start counts as a violation” and “cancellations within 2 hours incur a credit penalty.” Lawyers manage availability and accept or decline bookings; administrators monitor utilization and operational health through dashboards that summarize availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput volume, and closed-loop efficiency for alerts and incidents, with filters by organization, date range, and role. Reports can be exported locally to CSV/Excel and saved to disk with an on-screen confirmation; scheduled report “subscriptions” generate queued, local in-app notifications in the header inbox at 8:00 AM daily without relying on email, SMS, or push services. After a task is completed, both client and lawyer are prompted to submit a two-way review with 1–5 star ratings across timeliness, professionalism, and communication plus up to 1,000 characters of comments; disputes can be appealed within 7 days and routed into an arbitration flow visible to admins.

The backend uses Fastify to expose decoupled REST APIs consumed by the Angular frontend, with PostgreSQL for durable local persistence and auditability. Authentication is implemented with Spring-Security-like controls using JWT-style signed session tokens stored locally; cross-location login control is enforced by allowing one active session per user and expiring the prior session upon a new login, with configurable “admin exempt” rules. Authorization is tiered at both menu and API levels and further constrained by organization isolation, ensuring lawyers and clients only access their own matters while administrators can be scoped to a single organization or granted multi-tenant oversight. Booking concurrency is protected with a combination of database transactions and distributed locks implemented via PostgreSQL advisory locks, plus token-bucket rate limiting (for example, 20 booking writes per minute per user and 200 per minute per organization) to prevent overselling and to guarantee idempotency using client-generated request keys valid for 24 hours. A distributed job scheduling and queueing subsystem shards long-running tasks (such as incremental record crawling/import, report generation, and compliance checks) across multiple nodes, supports priorities, retries with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts, and idempotent execution guards; administrators can visually monitor job state, latency, and failure reasons. The rating and trust model maintains credit scores (0–100) with configurable rules such as -5 for late delivery, -10 for no-show, and +2 for five consecutive on-time completions, and every change is recorded with an explainable history visible to authorized staff. Integration is supported through offline-safe REST and optional local Webhook dispatch to on-prem endpoints, with configurable dictionaries and workflow steps stored in PostgreSQL; sensitive fields like notes and identifiers are encrypted at rest using AES-256 with a locally managed key, and logs mask personal data by default while retaining an immutable audit trail.

input.content:
# JusticeOps Offline Legal Services & Scheduling System — Implementation Plan

## Context
Build a full-stack offline-first legal services platform from scratch in an empty repo. Angular frontend + Fastify backend + PostgreSQL. The system coordinates legal casework, appointments, and compliance in air-gapped/low-connectivity environments with role-based access for Clients, Lawyers, and Administrators.

---

## Phase 1: Project Scaffolding & Database

### 1.1 Monorepo Setup
```
repo/
├── backend/                  # Fastify API
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app.ts           # Fastify app factory
│   │   ├── server.ts        # Entry point
│   │   ├── config/          # Environment & app config
│   │   ├── db/
│   │   │   ├── migrations/  # Knex migrations (numbered)
│   │   │   ├── seeds/       # Seed data
│   │   │   └── connection.ts
│   │   ├── plugins/         # Fastify plugins (auth, rate-limit, audit)
│   │   ├── modules/         # Feature modules (see below)
│   │   ├── middleware/       # Hooks, guards
│   │   ├── jobs/            # Job queue workers
│   │   └── utils/           # Encryption, masking, helpers
│   ├── knexfile.ts
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── frontend/                 # Angular app
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app/
│   │   │   ├── core/        # Guards, interceptors, services
│   │   │   ├── shared/      # Shared components, pipes, directives
│   │   │   ├── features/    # Feature modules by role
│   │   │   │   ├── auth/
│   │   │   │   ├── client/
│   │   │   │   ├── lawyer/
│   │   │   │   ├── admin/
│   │   │   │   ├── bookings/
│   │   │   │   ├── reviews/
│   │   │   │   ├── notifications/
│   │   │   │   └── reports/
│   │   │   ├── app.component.ts
│   │   │   ├── app.routes.ts
│   │   │   └── app.config.ts
│   │   └── environments/
│   ├── angular.json
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── docker-compose.yml        # PostgreSQL for local dev
└── README.md
```

### 1.2 Database Schema (Knex Migrations)

**Migration 001 — Core Tables:**
- `organizations` (id, name, settings_json, created_at)
- `users` (id, org_id FK, username, password_hash, role ENUM[client,lawyer,admin], credit_score DEFAULT 50, is_active, is_multi_tenant_admin, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, created_at, updated_at)
- `sessions` (id, user_id FK, token_jti, expires_at, created_at) — one active per user
- `idempotency_keys` (key, user_id, response_json, created_at, expires_at)

**Migration 002 — Booking Tables:**
- `availability` (id, lawyer_id FK, day_of_week, start_time, end_time, is_active)
- `bookings` (id, client_id FK, lawyer_id FK, org_id FK, type ENUM[consultation,milestone], status ENUM[pending,confirmed,completed,cancelled,no_show,rescheduled], scheduled_at, completed_at, cancellation_reason, idempotency_key, created_at, updated_at)

**Migration 003 — Reviews & Trust:**
- `reviews` (id, booking_id FK, reviewer_id FK, reviewee_id FK, timeliness_rating 1-5, professionalism_rating 1-5, communication_rating 1-5, comment VARCHAR(1000), created_at)
- `disputes` (id, review_id FK, appellant_id FK, reason, status ENUM[open,under_review,resolved,dismissed], resolution_notes, filed_at, resolved_at) — 7-day appeal window
- `credit_score_history` (id, user_id FK, change_amount, reason, rule_code, new_score, created_at)

**Migration 004 — Jobs & Notifications:**
- `jobs` (id, type, payload_json, priority, status ENUM[queued,running,completed,failed,dead], attempts, max_attempts DEFAULT 5, next_retry_at, last_error, shard_key, idempotency_key, created_at, started_at, completed_at)
- `notifications` (id, user_id FK, title, body, is_read DEFAULT false, created_at)
- `report_subscriptions` (id, user_id FK, report_type, filters_json, schedule_cron DEFAULT '0 8 * * *', is_active, created_at)

**Migration 005 — Security & Audit:**
- `audit_log` (id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, details_json, ip_address, created_at) — immutable, no UPDATE/DELETE
- `webhook_configs` (id, org_id FK, url, events_json, secret, is_active, created_at)
- `config_dictionaries` (id, org_id, category, key, value_json, created_at)
- `workflow_steps` (id, org_id, workflow_type, step_order, name, config_json)

**Sensitive field encryption:** `bookings.cancellation_reason`, user notes, identifiers — AES-256 via utility, key from env var.

### 1.3 Key Backend Packages
`fastify`, `@fastify/jwt`, `@fastify/cors`, `@fastify/rate-limit`, `knex`, `pg`, `bcrypt`, `uuid`, `node-cron`, `exceljs`, `json2csv`, `crypto` (built-in for AES-256)

---

## Phase 2: Backend API Modules

### 2.1 Authentication & Authorization (`modules/auth/`)
- **POST /api/auth/login** — validate password (bcrypt), check lockout (5 fails → 15min lock), issue JWT with jti, invalidate prior session, return token + role + menu permissions
- **POST /api/auth/logout** — invalidate session
- **Password validation** — 12+ chars, 1 number, 1 symbol
- **Middleware:** `authenticate` hook (verify JWT + session exists), `authorize(roles[])` hook, `orgIsolation` hook
- **Admin exempt config** for single-session rule

### 2.2 Users & Organizations (`modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/`)
- CRUD users (admin only), org-scoped queries
- GET /api/users/me — profile + credit score
- Org isolation enforced at query level (WHERE org_id = ?)

### 2.3 Bookings (`modules/bookings/`)
- **POST /api/bookings** — create with idempotency key, advisory lock on lawyer+timeslot (`pg_advisory_xact_lock`), rate-limit (20/min/user, 200/min/org)
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/reschedule** — with policy check (2h window)
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/cancel** — apply credit penalty if within 2h
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/confirm** — lawyer accepts
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/decline** — lawyer declines
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/complete** — triggers review prompt
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/no-show** — apply -10 credit, 10min grace period
- **GET /api/bookings** — filtered by role, status, date range

### 2.4 Availability (`modules/availability/`)
- CRUD for lawyer availability slots
- GET /api/availability/:lawyerId — public for booking

### 2.5 Reviews & Disputes (`modules/reviews/`)
- **POST /api/reviews** — 1-5 stars x3 categories + comment (1000 char max)
- **POST /api/disputes** — file within 7 days of review
- **PATCH /api/disputes/:id** — admin arbitration resolution
- **GET /api/disputes** — admin view with filters

### 2.6 Credit Scores (`modules/credit/`)
- GET /api/credit/:userId — score + history
- Internal service: `applyRule(userId, ruleCode)` — updates score, logs to history
- Configurable rules in `config_dictionaries`

### 2.7 Reports & Export (`modules/reports/`)
- **GET /api/reports/dashboard** — availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, closed-loop efficiency
- **GET /api/reports/export?format=csv|xlsx** — generate and return file
- **CRUD /api/report-subscriptions** — manage scheduled reports

### 2.8 Notifications (`modules/notifications/`)
- **GET /api/notifications** — paginated, unread count
- **PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read**
- Internal: `createNotification(userId, title, body)`

### 2.9 Jobs (`modules/jobs/`)
- **GET /api/jobs** — admin view with state/latency/failure filters
- **Worker process:** polls `jobs` table, claims with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, exponential backoff (attempt^2 * 1000ms), max 5 retries, idempotency guard
- **Job types:** report-generation, record-import, compliance-check
- **Cron:** node-cron runs at 8:00 AM → creates report-generation jobs for active subscriptions → on completion, inserts notification

### 2.10 Webhooks (`modules/webhooks/`)
- Internal dispatcher: on key events (booking created, completed, etc.), POST to configured org webhook URLs
- Fire-and-forget with retry (3 attempts), log results to audit

### 2.11 Audit & Security
- **Audit plugin:** Fastify onResponse hook logs all mutating requests to `audit_log`
- **Encryption util:** AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt for sensitive fields, key from `ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var
- **Log masking:** custom serializer strips PII from request/response logs

---

## Phase 3: Frontend (Angular)

### 3.1 Core (`core/`)
- `auth.service.ts` — login, logout, token storage, role getter
- `auth.interceptor.ts` — attach JWT to requests
- `error.interceptor.ts` — handle 401 (redirect login), 403 (permission toast), 429 (rate limit feedback)
- `auth.guard.ts` — route guard checking role
- `notification.service.ts` — poll GET /api/notifications on interval
- `idempotency.service.ts` — generate UUID keys for booking requests

### 3.2 Shared (`shared/`)
- `role-menu.component.ts` — dynamic navigation based on user role
- `permission-feedback.component.ts` — inline "You don't have permission" messages
- `star-rating.component.ts` — reusable 1-5 star input
- `confirm-dialog.component.ts` — policy confirmation modals
- `notification-bell.component.ts` — header inbox with badge
- `export-button.component.ts` — CSV/Excel download with confirmation toast

### 3.3 Feature: Auth (`features/auth/`)
- `login.component.ts` — form with validation, lockout message display
- `login.component.html` — password rules hint, error states

### 3.4 Feature: Client Workspace (`features/client/`)
- `client-dashboard.component.ts` — upcoming bookings, credit score summary
- `booking-create.component.ts` — form with lawyer/time selection, policy display
- `booking-list.component.ts` — status tracking, reschedule/cancel actions
- `booking-detail.component.ts` — full details, policy messages
- `credit-history.component.ts` — score + explainable changes

### 3.5 Feature: Lawyer Workspace (`features/lawyer/`)
- `lawyer-dashboard.component.ts` — pending bookings, today's schedule
- `availability-manager.component.ts` — set/edit availability slots
- `booking-requests.component.ts` — accept/decline incoming bookings

### 3.6 Feature: Admin Workspace (`features/admin/`)
- `admin-dashboard.component.ts` — summary cards (availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, efficiency)
- `dashboard-filters.component.ts` — org, date range, role filters
- `job-monitor.component.ts` — job list with state, latency, failure reasons
- `user-management.component.ts` — CRUD users
- `org-management.component.ts` — CRUD organizations
- `arbitration.component.ts` — dispute list, resolution form

### 3.7 Feature: Reviews (`features/reviews/`)
- `review-form.component.ts` — 3 star categories + comment (1000 char counter)
- `review-list.component.ts` — past reviews given/received
- `dispute-form.component.ts` — file appeal (within 7 days)

### 3.8 Feature: Reports (`features/reports/`)
- `report-viewer.component.ts` — dashboard visualizations
- `report-export.component.ts` — download with format selection + confirmation
- `subscription-manager.component.ts` — manage scheduled report subscriptions

### 3.9 Feature: Notifications (`features/notifications/`)
- `notification-list.component.ts` — full notification inbox
- Integrated into `notification-bell.component.ts` in header

### 3.10 Routing (`app.routes.ts`)
```
/login
/client/*          (guard: role=client)
/lawyer/*          (guard: role=lawyer)
/admin/*           (guard: role=admin)
/bookings/*        (guard: authenticated)
/reviews/*         (guard: authenticated)
/notifications     (guard: authenticated)
```
All feature routes lazy-loaded.

---

## Phase 4: Implementation Order

| Step | What | Files |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | Init monorepo, package.json, tsconfig, docker-compose | Root configs |
| 2 | Backend scaffolding: Fastify app, config, DB connection | `backend/src/app.ts`, `server.ts`, `config/`, `db/` |
| 3 | DB migrations (all 5) | `backend/src/db/migrations/` |
| 4 | Auth module (login, JWT, session, lockout, password rules) | `backend/src/modules/auth/` |
| 5 | Auth middleware (authenticate, authorize, orgIsolation) | `backend/src/plugins/`, `backend/src/middleware/` |
| 6 | Users & Organizations CRUD | `backend/src/modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/` |
| 7 | Availability & Bookings (with advisory locks, rate limiting, idempotency) | `backend/src/modules/availability/`, `modules/bookings/` |
| 8 | Reviews, Disputes, Credit Scores | `backend/src/modules/reviews/`, `modules/credit/` |
| 9 | Jobs queue, worker, cron scheduler | `backend/src/jobs/`, `modules/jobs/` |
| 10 | Reports, export, subscriptions | `backend/src/modules/reports/` |
| 11 | Notifications | `backend/src/modules/notifications/` |
| 12 | Webhooks, audit plugin, encryption util | `backend/src/modules/webhooks/`, `plugins/audit.ts`, `utils/` |
| 13 | Angular scaffolding, core services, interceptors, guards | `frontend/src/app/core/` |
| 14 | Shared components (menu, star-rating, dialogs, notification bell) | `frontend/src/app/shared/` |
| 15 | Auth feature (login page) | `frontend/src/app/features/auth/` |
| 16 | Client workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/client/` |
| 17 | Lawyer workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/lawyer/` |
| 18 | Admin workspace (dashboards, job monitor, arbitration) | `frontend/src/app/features/admin/` |
| 19 | Reviews & Reports features | `frontend/src/app/features/reviews/`, `features/reports/` |
| 20 | Notifications feature | `frontend/src/app/features/notifications/` |
| 21 | Seed data & end-to-end smoke test | Seeds, manual testing |

---

## Verification
1. `docker-compose up` — PostgreSQL running
2. `cd backend && npm run migrate && npm run dev` — API on :3000
3. `cd frontend && ng serve` — UI on :4200
4. Create admin user via seed → login → verify JWT + session
5. Create lawyer + client → test booking flow with concurrency (two simultaneous requests)
6. Complete booking → verify review prompts appear for both parties
7. File dispute → verify admin arbitration view
8. Check admin dashboard metrics populate
9. Create report subscription → verify 8AM job creates notification
10. Export report → verify CSV/Excel downloads with confirmation

The system coordinates legal casework, appointments, and compliance in air-gapped/low-connectivity environments with role-based access for Clients, Lawyers, and Administrators.

### 1.1 Monorepo Setup

├── backend/                  # Fastify API
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app.ts           # Fastify app factory
│   │   ├── server.ts        # Entry point
│   │   ├── config/          # Environment & app config

│   │   │   └── connection.ts
│   │   ├── plugins/         # Fastify plugins (auth, rate-limit, audit)
│   │   ├── modules/         # Feature modules (see below)
│   │   ├── middleware/       # Hooks, guards
│   │   ├── jobs/            # Job queue workers
│   │   └── utils/           # Encryption, masking, helpers
│   ├── knexfile.ts
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── frontend/                 # Angular app
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app/
│   │   │   ├── core/        # Guards, interceptors, services
│   │   │   ├── shared/      # Shared components, pipes, directives
│   │   │   ├── features/    # Feature modules by role
│   │   │   │   ├── auth/
│   │   │   │   ├── client/
│   │   │   │   ├── lawyer/
│   │   │   │   ├── admin/
│   │   │   │   ├── bookings/
│   │   │   │   ├── reviews/
│   │   │   │   ├── notifications/
│   │   │   │   └── reports/
│   │   │   ├── app.component.ts
│   │   │   ├── app.routes.ts
│   │   │   └── app.config.ts
│   │   └── environments/
│   ├── angular.json
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── docker-compose.yml        # PostgreSQL for local dev
└── README.md
```

### 1.2 Database Schema (Knex Migrations)

**Migration 001 — Core Tables:**
- `organizations` (id, name, settings_json, created_at)
- `users` (id, org_id FK, username, password_hash, role ENUM[client,lawyer,admin], credit_score DEFAULT 50, is_active, is_multi_tenant_admin, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, created_at, updated_at)
- `sessions` (id, user_id FK, token_jti, expires_at, created_at) — one active per user
- `idempotency_keys` (key, user_id, response_json, created_at, expires_at)

**Migration 002 — Booking Tables:**
- `availability` (id, lawyer_id FK, day_of_week, start_time, end_time, is_active)
- `bookings` (id, client_id FK, lawyer_id FK, org_id FK, type ENUM[consultation,milestone], status ENUM[pending,confirmed,completed,cancelled,no_show,rescheduled], scheduled_at, completed_at, cancellation_reason, idempotency_key, created_at, updated_at)

**Migration 003 — Reviews & Trust:**
- `reviews` (id, booking_id FK, reviewer_id FK, reviewee_id FK, timeliness_rating 1-5, professionalism_rating 1-5, communication_rating 1-5, comment VARCHAR(1000), created_at)
- `disputes` (id, review_id FK, appellant_id FK, reason, status ENUM[open,under_review,resolved,dismissed], resolution_notes, filed_at, resolved_at) — 7-day appeal window
- `credit_score_history` (id, user_id FK, change_amount, reason, rule_code, new_score, created_at)

**Migration 004 — Jobs & Notifications:**
- `jobs` (id, type, payload_json, priority, status ENUM[queued,running,completed,failed,dead], attempts, max_attempts DEFAULT 5, next_retry_at, last_error, shard_key, idempotency_key, created_at, started_at, completed_at)
- `notifications` (id, user_id FK, title, body, is_read DEFAULT false, created_at)
- `report_subscriptions` (id, user_id FK, report_type, filters_json, schedule_cron DEFAULT '0 8 * * *', is_active, created_at)

**Migration 005 — Security & Audit:**
- `audit_log` (id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, details_json, ip_address, created_at) — immutable, no UPDATE/DELETE
- `webhook_configs` (id, org_id FK, url, events_json, secret, is_active, created_at)
- `config_dictionaries` (id, org_id, category, key, value_json, created_at)
- `workflow_steps` (id, org_id, workflow_type, step_order, name, config_json)

**Sensitive field encryption:** `bookings.cancellation_reason`, user notes, identifiers — AES-256 via utility, key from env var.

### 1.3 Key Backend Packages
`fastify`, `@fastify/jwt`, `@fastify/cors`, `@fastify/rate-limit`, `knex`, `pg`, `bcrypt`, `uuid`, `node-cron`, `exceljs`, `json2csv`, `crypto` (built-in for AES-256)

---

## Phase 2: Backend API Modules

### 2.1 Authentication & Authorization (`modules/auth/`)
- **POST /api/auth/login** — validate password (bcrypt), check lockout (5 fails → 15min lock), issue JWT with jti, invalidate prior session, return token + role + menu permissions
- **POST /api/auth/logout** — invalidate session
- **Password validation** — 12+ chars, 1 number, 1 symbol
- **Middleware:** `authenticate` hook (verify JWT + session exists), `authorize(roles[])` hook, `orgIsolation` hook
- **Admin exempt config** for single-session rule

### 2.2 Users & Organizations (`modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/`)
- CRUD users (admin only), org-scoped queries
- GET /api/users/me — profile + credit score
- Org isolation enforced at query level (WHERE org_id = ?)

### 2.3 Bookings (`modules/bookings/`)
- **POST /api/bookings** — create with idempotency key, advisory lock on lawyer+timeslot (`pg_advisory_xact_lock`), rate-limit (20/min/user, 200/min/org)
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/reschedule** — with policy check (2h window)
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/cancel** — apply credit penalty if within 2h
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/confirm** — lawyer accepts
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/decline** — lawyer declines
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/complete** — triggers review prompt
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/no-show** — apply -10 credit, 10min grace period
- **GET /api/bookings** — filtered by role, status, date range

### 2.4 Availability (`modules/availability/`)
- CRUD for lawyer availability slots
- GET /api/availability/:lawyerId — public for booking

### 2.5 Reviews & Disputes (`modules/reviews/`)
- **POST /api/reviews** — 1-5 stars x3 categories + comment (1000 char max)
- **POST /api/disputes** — file within 7 days of review
- **PATCH /api/disputes/:id** — admin arbitration resolution
- **GET /api/disputes** — admin view with filters

### 2.6 Credit Scores (`modules/credit/`)
- GET /api/credit/:userId — score + history
- Internal service: `applyRule(userId, ruleCode)` — updates score, logs to history
- Configurable rules in `config_dictionaries`

### 2.7 Reports & Export (`modules/reports/`)
- **GET /api/reports/dashboard** — availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, closed-loop efficiency
- **GET /api/reports/export?format=csv|xlsx** — generate and return file
- **CRUD /api/report-subscriptions** — manage scheduled reports

### 2.8 Notifications (`modules/notifications/`)
- **GET /api/notifications** — paginated, unread count
- **PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read**
- Internal: `createNotification(userId, title, body)`

### 2.9 Jobs (`modules/jobs/`)
- **GET /api/jobs** — admin view with state/latency/failure filters
- **Worker process:** polls `jobs` table, claims with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, exponential backoff (attempt^2 * 1000ms), max 5 retries, idempotency guard
- **Job types:** report-generation, record-import, compliance-check
- **Cron:** node-cron runs at 8:00 AM → creates report-generation jobs for active subscriptions → on completion, inserts notification

### 2.10 Webhooks (`modules/webhooks/`)
- Internal dispatcher: on key events (booking created, completed, etc.), POST to configured org webhook URLs
- Fire-and-forget with retry (3 attempts), log results to audit

### 2.11 Audit & Security
- **Audit plugin:** Fastify onResponse hook logs all mutating requests to `audit_log`
- **Encryption util:** AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt for sensitive fields, key from `ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var
- **Log masking:** custom serializer strips PII from request/response logs

---

## Phase 3: Frontend (Angular)

### 3.1 Core (`core/`)
- `auth.service.ts` — login, logout, token storage, role getter
- `auth.interceptor.ts` — attach JWT to requests
- `error.interceptor.ts` — handle 401 (redirect login), 403 (permission toast), 429 (rate limit feedback)
- `auth.guard.ts` — route guard checking role
- `notification.service.ts` — poll GET /api/notifications on interval
- `idempotency.service.ts` — generate UUID keys for booking requests

### 3.2 Shared (`shared/`)
- `role-menu.component.ts` — dynamic navigation based on user role
- `permission-feedback.component.ts` — inline "You don't have permission" messages
- `star-rating.component.ts` — reusable 1-5 star input
- `confirm-dialog.component.ts` — policy confirmation modals
- `notification-bell.component.ts` — header inbox with badge
- `export-button.component.ts` — CSV/Excel download with confirmation toast

### 3.3 Feature: Auth (`features/auth/`)
- `login.component.ts` — form with validation, lockout message display
- `login.component.html` — password rules hint, error states

### 3.4 Feature: Client Workspace (`features/client/`)
- `client-dashboard.component.ts` — upcoming bookings, credit score summary
- `booking-create.component.ts` — form with lawyer/time selection, policy display
- `booking-list.component.ts` — status tracking, reschedule/cancel actions
- `booking-detail.component.ts` — full details, policy messages
- `credit-history.component.ts` — score + explainable changes

### 3.5 Feature: Lawyer Workspace (`features/lawyer/`)
- `lawyer-dashboard.component.ts` — pending bookings, today's schedule
- `availability-manager.component.ts` — set/edit availability slots
- `booking-requests.component.ts` — accept/decline incoming bookings

### 3.6 Feature: Admin Workspace (`features/admin/`)
- `admin-dashboard.component.ts` — summary cards (availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, efficiency)
- `dashboard-filters.component.ts` — org, date range, role filters
- `job-monitor.component.ts` — job list with state, latency, failure reasons
- `user-management.component.ts` — CRUD users
- `org-management.component.ts` — CRUD organizations
- `arbitration.component.ts` — dispute list, resolution form

### 3.7 Feature: Reviews (`features/reviews/`)
- `review-form.component.ts` — 3 star categories + comment (1000 char counter)
- `review-list.component.ts` — past reviews given/received
- `dispute-form.component.ts` — file appeal (within 7 days)

### 3.8 Feature: Reports (`features/reports/`)
- `report-viewer.component.ts` — dashboard visualizations
- `report-export.component.ts` — download with format selection + confirmation
- `subscription-manager.component.ts` — manage scheduled report subscriptions

### 3.9 Feature: Notifications (`features/notifications/`)
- `notification-list.component.ts` — full notification inbox
- Integrated into `notification-bell.component.ts` in header

### 3.10 Routing (`app.routes.ts`)
```
/login
/client/*          (guard: role=client)
/lawyer/*          (guard: role=lawyer)
/admin/*           (guard: role=admin)
/bookings/*        (guard: authenticated)
/reviews/*         (guard: authenticated)
/notifications     (guard: authenticated)
```
All feature routes lazy-loaded.

---

## Phase 4: Implementation Order

| Step | What | Files |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | Init monorepo, package.json, tsconfig, docker-compose | Root configs |
| 2 | Backend scaffolding: Fastify app, config, DB connection | `backend/src/app.ts`, `server.ts`, `config/`, `db/` |
| 3 | DB migrations (all 5) | `backend/src/db/migrations/` |
| 4 | Auth module (login, JWT, session, lockout, password rules) | `backend/src/modules/auth/` |
| 5 | Auth middleware (authenticate, authorize, orgIsolation) | `backend/src/plugins/`, `backend/src/middleware/` |
| 6 | Users & Organizations CRUD | `backend/src/modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/` |
| 7 | Availability & Bookings (with advisory locks, rate limiting, idempotency) | `backend/src/modules/availability/`, `modules/bookings/` |
| 8 | Reviews, Disputes, Credit Scores | `backend/src/modules/reviews/`, `modules/credit/` |
| 9 | Jobs queue, worker, cron scheduler | `backend/src/jobs/`, `modules/jobs/` |
| 10 | Reports, export, subscriptions | `backend/src/modules/reports/` |
| 11 | Notifications | `backend/src/modules/notifications/` |
| 12 | Webhooks, audit plugin, encryption util | `backend/src/modules/webhooks/`, `plugins/audit.ts`, `utils/` |
| 13 | Angular scaffolding, core services, interceptors, guards | `frontend/src/app/core/` |
| 14 | Shared components (menu, star-rating, dialogs, notification bell) | `frontend/src/app/shared/` |
| 15 | Auth feature (login page) | `frontend/src/app/features/auth/` |
| 16 | Client workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/client/` |
| 17 | Lawyer workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/lawyer/` |
| 18 | Admin workspace (dashboards, job monitor, arbitration) | `frontend/src/app/features/admin/` |
| 19 | Reviews & Reports features | `frontend/src/app/features/reviews/`, `features/reports/` |
| 20 | Notifications feature | `frontend/src/app/features/notifications/` |
| 21 | Seed data & end-to-end smoke test | Seeds, manual testing |

---

## Verification
1. `docker-compose up` — PostgreSQL running
2. `cd backend && npm run migrate && npm run dev` — API on :3000
3. `cd frontend && ng serve` — UI on :4200
4. Create admin user via seed → login → verify JWT + session
5. Create lawyer + client → test booking flow with concurrency (two simultaneous requests)
6. Complete booking → verify review prompts appear for both parties
7. File dispute → verify admin arbitration view
8. Check admin dashboard metrics populate
9. Create report subscription → verify 8AM job creates notification
10. Export report → verify CSV/Excel downloads with confirmation

content.text:
The plan covers the full system across 21 implementation steps — backend (Fastify + PostgreSQL with auth, bookings, jobs, reviews, credit scores, audit) and frontend (Angular with role-based workspaces for Client, Lawyer, Admin). Key architectural decisions:

- **Knex** for migrations/queries (lighter than Prisma for offline/air-gapped)
- **PostgreSQL advisory locks** for booking concurrency
- **node-cron** for the 8AM report subscription scheduler
- **FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED** pattern for distributed job queue
- **AES-256-GCM** for field-level encryption of sensitive data
- **Lazy-loaded Angular routes** per role with standalone components

allowedPrompts.prompt: run Angular CLI commands (ng generate, ng new)

# JusticeOps Offline Legal Services & Scheduling System — Implementation Plan

## Context
Build a full-stack offline-first legal services platform from scratch in an empty repo. Angular frontend + Fastify backend + PostgreSQL. The system coordinates legal casework, appointments, and compliance in air-gapped/low-connectivity environments with role-based access for Clients, Lawyers, and Administrators.

---

│   ├── src/

│   │   ├── config/          # Environment & app config
│   │   ├── db/
│   │   │   ├── migrations/  # Knex migrations (numbered)
│   │   │   ├── seeds/       # Seed data
│   │   │   └── connection.ts
│   │   ├── plugins/         # Fastify plugins (auth, rate-limit, audit)

│   └── tsconfig.json

- `report-viewer.component.ts` — dashboard visualizations

- `subscription-manager.component.ts` — manage scheduled report subscriptions

### 3.9 Feature: Notifications (`features/notifications/`)
- `notification-list.component.ts` — full notification inbox
- Integrated into `notification-bell.component.ts` in header

### 3.10 Routing (`app.routes.ts`)
```
/login
/client/*          (guard: role=client)
/lawyer/*          (guard: role=lawyer)
/admin/*           (guard: role=admin)
/bookings/*        (guard: authenticated)
/reviews/*         (guard: authenticated)
/notifications     (guard: authenticated)
```
All feature routes lazy-loaded.

---

## Phase 4: Implementation Order

| Step | What | Files |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | Init monorepo, package.json, tsconfig, docker-compose | Root configs |
| 2 | Backend scaffolding: Fastify app, config, DB connection | `backend/src/app.ts`, `server.ts`, `config/`, `db/` |
| 3 | DB migrations (all 5) | `backend/src/db/migrations/` |
| 4 | Auth module (login, JWT, session, lockout, password rules) | `backend/src/modules/auth/` |
| 5 | Auth middleware (authenticate, authorize, orgIsolation) | `backend/src/plugins/`, `backend/src/middleware/` |
| 6 | Users & Organizations CRUD | `backend/src/modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/` |
| 7 | Availability & Bookings (with advisory locks, rate limiting, idempotency) | `backend/src/modules/availability/`, `modules/bookings/` |
| 8 | Reviews, Disputes, Credit Scores | `backend/src/modules/reviews/`, `modules/credit/` |
| 9 | Jobs queue, worker, cron scheduler | `backend/src/jobs/`, `modules/jobs/` |
| 10 | Reports, export, subscriptions | `backend/src/modules/reports/` |
| 11 | Notifications | `backend/src/modules/notifications/` |
| 12 | Webhooks, audit plugin, encryption util | `backend/src/modules/webhooks/`, `plugins/audit.ts`, `utils/` |
| 13 | Angular scaffolding, core services, interceptors, guards | `frontend/src/app/core/` |
| 14 | Shared components (menu, star-rating, dialogs, notification bell) | `frontend/src/app/shared/` |
| 15 | Auth feature (login page) | `frontend/src/app/features/auth/` |
| 16 | Client workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/client/` |
| 17 | Lawyer workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/lawyer/` |
| 18 | Admin workspace (dashboards, job monitor, arbitration) | `frontend/src/app/features/admin/` |
| 19 | Reviews & Reports features | `frontend/src/app/features/reviews/`, `features/reports/` |
| 20 | Notifications feature | `frontend/src/app/features/notifications/` |
| 21 | Seed data & end-to-end smoke test | Seeds, manual testing |

---

## Verification
1. `docker-compose up` — PostgreSQL running
2. `cd backend && npm run migrate && npm run dev` — API on :3000
3. `cd frontend && ng serve` — UI on :4200
4. Create admin user via seed → login → verify JWT + session
5. Create lawyer + client → test booking flow with concurrency (two simultaneous requests)
6. Complete booking → verify review prompts appear for both parties
7. File dispute → verify admin arbitration view
8. Check admin dashboard metrics populate
9. Create report subscription → verify 8AM job creates notification
10. Export report → verify CSV/Excel downloads with confirmation

message.content:
alright now I want you to consider the following prompt and update the plan of action: . System Overview & Environment
Develop JusticeOps, a legal coordination system designed for air-gapped, low-connectivity environments. The system must operate entirely on a Local Area Network (LAN) without access to external APIs, Cloud KMS, CDN, or public NTP servers.

Tech Stack: Frontend: Angular (Standalone components); Backend: Fastify (Node.js) with decoupled REST APIs; Database: PostgreSQL (Primary persistence, job queue, and audit engine).

2. Authentication & Session Security

Identity: Local username/password verification with rules requiring at least 12 characters, including one number and one symbol.

Lockout: Automatic 15-minute lockout after 5 failed login attempts.

Session Control: Implement JWT-style signed session tokens stored locally.

Cross-Location Control: Enforce exactly one active session per user. Store a session_nonce in a user_sessions table. Fastify must validate the nonce on every request; a new login at a different workstation must revoke the previous nonce.

Admin Exemption: Configurable rules to allow specific "admin exempt" accounts to maintain multiple concurrent sessions.

3. Organizational Multi-Tenancy & Authorization

Data Isolation: Implement Row-Level Security (RLS) or mandatory org_id filtering at the database level. Ensure lawyers and clients only access their own matters.

Admin Scoping: Support single-organization admins and multi-tenant "Super Admins."

Feedback: UI must provide in-context permission feedback (e.g., "Action Restricted: Requires Admin Role") when actions are blocked by role-based logic.

4. Scheduling & Concurrency Engine

Booking Types:

Consultations: Specific time-blocked slots (e.g., 60-minute windows).

Case Milestones: Capacity-based deadlines. These do not block a specific hour but reduce a lawyer's total daily "Workload Capacity" via weight-based logic.

Concurrency & Idempotency:

Use PostgreSQL Advisory Locks (pg_advisory_xact_lock) combined with transactions to prevent double-booking high-contention slots.

Guarantee idempotency using client-generated request keys valid for 24 hours. Store response payloads in an idempotency_registry table.

Rate Limiting: Implement a Token-Bucket algorithm persisted in a rate_limit_buckets table (e.g., 20 writes/min per user; 200/min per org) to ensure synchronization across distributed Fastify nodes.

5. Offline Business Rules & Trust Model

Time Synchronization: The Angular UI must sync with the Backend/Database time on handshake to calculate offsets. All business-critical time calculations (lockouts, 10-minute no-shows, 2-hour cancellation windows) must be calculated using the server’s clock, not the client's workstation.

Credit Score (0–100):

Rules: -5 for late delivery, -10 for no-show, +2 for five consecutive on-time completions.

Throttling: If a score falls below a threshold (e.g., 20), the UI must disable "New Booking" actions, and the API must return a 403 "Account Under Review" status.

Reviews & Arbitration:

Two-way 1–5 star reviews (Timeliness, Professionalism, Communication) plus up to 1,000 character comments.

Dispute Flow: Appeals filed within 7 days move penalties into a "Pending/Escrow" state. Scores are only updated once an Admin rules on the arbitration.

6. Distributed Jobs & Reporting

Job Subsystem: No external brokers (Redis/RabbitMQ). Use a Transactional Outbox pattern in PostgreSQL. Shard tasks (record crawling, report generation, compliance checks) across nodes using SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED.

Idempotency Cleanup: A daily 2:00 AM vacuum job must purge idempotency keys older than 24 hours.

Notifications & Reports:

Generate queued, local in-app notifications in the header inbox at 8:00 AM daily for subscriptions.

Jitter Logic: Stagger 8:00 AM notification generation across a 15-minute window to prevent database CPU/IO spikes.

Export reports locally to CSV/Excel and save to disk with an on-screen confirmation message.

7. Security & Compliance

At-Rest Encryption: Encrypt sensitive fields like notes and identifiers using AES-256.

Key Management: Master Key provided via environment variable. The system must force a "Paper Backup" confirmation for Admins on first initialization. Use pre-save hooks in the backend for transparent encryption.

Auditing & Masking:

App Logs: Redact/mask PII (names, phones) in stdout logs for developers.

Audit Trail: Maintain an immutable audit_log table containing full, unmasked JSONB states (OLD/NEW), accessible only by authorized Super Admin roles.

8. Frontend Workspaces

Client Workspace: Focus on booking management, credit status visibility, and clear policy messages (e.g., no-show and cancellation penalty warnings).

Lawyer Workspace: Focus on availability management, booking triage, and workload capacity views.

Admin Workspace: Dashboards for operational health monitoring availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput volume, and closed-loop efficiency. Include filters for organization, date range, and role.

1	# JusticeOps Offline Legal Services & Scheduling System — Implementation Plan
2	
3	## Context
4	Build a full-stack offline-first legal services platform from scratch in an empty repo. Angular frontend + Fastify backend + PostgreSQL. The system coordinates legal casework, appointments, and compliance in air-gapped/low-connectivity environments with role-based access for Clients, Lawyers, and Administrators.
5	
6	---
7	
8	## Phase 1: Project Scaffolding & Database
9	
10	### 1.1 Monorepo Setup
11	```
12	repo/
13	├── backend/                  # Fastify API
14	│   ├── src/
15	│   │   ├── app.ts           # Fastify app factory
16	│   │   ├── server.ts        # Entry point
17	│   │   ├── config/          # Environment & app config
18	│   │   ├── db/
19	│   │   │   ├── migrations/  # Knex migrations (numbered)
20	│   │   │   ├── seeds/       # Seed data
21	│   │   │   └── connection.ts
22	│   │   ├── plugins/         # Fastify plugins (auth, rate-limit, audit)
23	│   │   ├── modules/         # Feature modules (see below)
24	│   │   ├── middleware/       # Hooks, guards
25	│   │   ├── jobs/            # Job queue workers
26	│   │   └── utils/           # Encryption, masking, helpers
27	│   ├── knexfile.ts
28	│   ├── package.json
29	│   └── tsconfig.json
30	├── frontend/                 # Angular app
31	│   ├── src/
32	│   │   ├── app/
33	│   │   │   ├── core/        # Guards, interceptors, services
34	│   │   │   ├── shared/      # Shared components, pipes, directives
35	│   │   │   ├── features/    # Feature modules by role
36	│   │   │   │   ├── auth/
37	│   │   │   │   ├── client/
38	│   │   │   │   ├── lawyer/
39	│   │   │   │   ├── admin/
40	│   │   │   │   ├── bookings/
41	│   │   │   │   ├── reviews/
42	│   │   │   │   ├── notifications/
43	│   │   │   │   └── reports/
44	│   │   │   ├── app.component.ts
45	│   │   │   ├── app.routes.ts
46	│   │   │   └── app.config.ts
47	│   │   └── environments/
48	│   ├── angular.json
49	│   ├── package.json
50	│   └── tsconfig.json
51	├── docker-compose.yml        # PostgreSQL for local dev
52	└── README.md
53	```
54	
55	### 1.2 Database Schema (Knex Migrations)
56	
57	**Migration 001 — Core Tables:**
58	- `organizations` (id, name, settings_json, created_at)
59	- `users` (id, org_id FK, username, password_hash, role ENUM[client,lawyer,admin], credit_score DEFAULT 50, is_active, is_multi_tenant_admin, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, created_at, updated_at)
60	- `sessions` (id, user_id FK, token_jti, expires_at, created_at) — one active per user
61	- `idempotency_keys` (key, user_id, response_json, created_at, expires_at)
62	
63	**Migration 002 — Booking Tables:**
64	- `availability` (id, lawyer_id FK, day_of_week, start_time, end_time, is_active)
65	- `bookings` (id, client_id FK, lawyer_id FK, org_id FK, type ENUM[consultation,milestone], status ENUM[pending,confirmed,completed,cancelled,no_show,rescheduled], scheduled_at, completed_at, cancellation_reason, idempotency_key, created_at, updated_at)
66	
67	**Migration 003 — Reviews & Trust:**
68	- `reviews` (id, booking_id FK, reviewer_id FK, reviewee_id FK, timeliness_rating 1-5, professionalism_rating 1-5, communication_rating 1-5, comment VARCHAR(1000), created_at)
69	- `disputes` (id, review_id FK, appellant_id FK, reason, status ENUM[open,under_review,resolved,dismissed], resolution_notes, filed_at, resolved_at) — 7-day appeal window
70	- `credit_score_history` (id, user_id FK, change_amount, reason, rule_code, new_score, created_at)
71	
72	**Migration 004 — Jobs & Notifications:**
73	- `jobs` (id, type, payload_json, priority, status ENUM[queued,running,completed,failed,dead], attempts, max_attempts DEFAULT 5, next_retry_at, last_error, shard_key, idempotency_key, created_at, started_at, completed_at)
74	- `notifications` (id, user_id FK, title, body, is_read DEFAULT false, created_at)
75	- `report_subscriptions` (id, user_id FK, report_type, filters_json, schedule_cron DEFAULT '0 8 * * *', is_active, created_at)
76	
77	**Migration 005 — Security & Audit:**
78	- `audit_log` (id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, details_json, ip_address, created_at) — immutable, no UPDATE/DELETE
79	- `webhook_configs` (id, org_id FK, url, events_json, secret, is_active, created_at)
80	- `config_dictionaries` (id, org_id, category, key, value_json, created_at)
81	- `workflow_steps` (id, org_id, workflow_type, step_order, name, config_json)
82	
83	**Sensitive field encryption:** `bookings.cancellation_reason`, user notes, identifiers — AES-256 via utility, key from env var.
84	
85	### 1.3 Key Backend Packages
86	`fastify`, `@fastify/jwt`, `@fastify/cors`, `@fastify/rate-limit`, `knex`, `pg`, `bcrypt`, `uuid`, `node-cron`, `exceljs`, `json2csv`, `crypto` (built-in for AES-256)
87	
88	---
89	
90	## Phase 2: Backend API Modules
91	
92	### 2.1 Authentication & Authorization (`modules/auth/`)
93	- **POST /api/auth/login** — validate password (bcrypt), check lockout (5 fails → 15min lock), issue JWT with jti, invalidate prior session, return token + role + menu permissions
94	- **POST /api/auth/logout** — invalidate session
95	- **Password validation** — 12+ chars, 1 number, 1 symbol
96	- **Middleware:** `authenticate` hook (verify JWT + session exists), `authorize(roles[])` hook, `orgIsolation` hook
97	- **Admin exempt config** for single-session rule
98	
99	### 2.2 Users & Organizations (`modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/`)
100	- CRUD users (admin only), org-scoped queries
101	- GET /api/users/me — profile + credit score
102	- Org isolation enforced at query level (WHERE org_id = ?)
103	
104	### 2.3 Bookings (`modules/bookings/`)
105	- **POST /api/bookings** — create with idempotency key, advisory lock on lawyer+timeslot (`pg_advisory_xact_lock`), rate-limit (20/min/user, 200/min/org)
106	- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/reschedule** — with policy check (2h window)
107	- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/cancel** — apply credit penalty if within 2h
108	- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/confirm** — lawyer accepts
109	- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/decline** — lawyer declines
110	- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/complete** — triggers review prompt
111	- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/no-show** — apply -10 credit, 10min grace period
112	- **GET /api/bookings** — filtered by role, status, date range
113	
114	### 2.4 Availability (`modules/availability/`)
115	- CRUD for lawyer availability slots
116	- GET /api/availability/:lawyerId — public for booking
117	
118	### 2.5 Reviews & Disputes (`modules/reviews/`)
119	- **POST /api/reviews** — 1-5 stars x3 categories + comment (1000 char max)
120	- **POST /api/disputes** — file within 7 days of review
121	- **PATCH /api/disputes/:id** — admin arbitration resolution
122	- **GET /api/disputes** — admin view with filters
123	
124	### 2.6 Credit Scores (`modules/credit/`)
125	- GET /api/credit/:userId — score + history
126	- Internal service: `applyRule(userId, ruleCode)` — updates score, logs to history
127	- Configurable rules in `config_dictionaries`
128	
129	### 2.7 Reports & Export (`modules/reports/`)
130	- **GET /api/reports/dashboard** — availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, closed-loop efficiency
131	- **GET /api/reports/export?format=csv|xlsx** — generate and return file
132	- **CRUD /api/report-subscriptions** — manage scheduled reports
133	
134	### 2.8 Notifications (`modules/notifications/`)
135	- **GET /api/notifications** — paginated, unread count
136	- **PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read**
137	- Internal: `createNotification(userId, title, body)`
138	
139	### 2.9 Jobs (`modules/jobs/`)
140	- **GET /api/jobs** — admin view with state/latency/failure filters
141	- **Worker process:** polls `jobs` table, claims with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, exponential backoff (attempt^2 * 1000ms), max 5 retries, idempotency guard
142	- **Job types:** report-generation, record-import, compliance-check
143	- **Cron:** node-cron runs at 8:00 AM → creates report-generation jobs for active subscriptions → on completion, inserts notification
144	
145	### 2.10 Webhooks (`modules/webhooks/`)
146	- Internal dispatcher: on key events (booking created, completed, etc.), POST to configured org webhook URLs
147	- Fire-and-forget with retry (3 attempts), log results to audit
148	
149	### 2.11 Audit & Security
150	- **Audit plugin:** Fastify onResponse hook logs all mutating requests to `audit_log`
151	- **Encryption util:** AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt for sensitive fields, key from `ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var
152	- **Log masking:** custom serializer strips PII from request/response logs
153	
154	---
155	
156	## Phase 3: Frontend (Angular)
157	
158	### 3.1 Core (`core/`)
159	- `auth.service.ts` — login, logout, token storage, role getter
160	- `auth.interceptor.ts` — attach JWT to requests
161	- `error.interceptor.ts` — handle 401 (redirect login), 403 (permission toast), 429 (rate limit feedback)
162	- `auth.guard.ts` — route guard checking role
163	- `notification.service.ts` — poll GET /api/notifications on interval
164	- `idempotency.service.ts` — generate UUID keys for booking requests
165	
166	### 3.2 Shared (`shared/`)
167	- `role-menu.component.ts` — dynamic navigation based on user role
168	- `permission-feedback.component.ts` — inline "You don't have permission" messages
169	- `star-rating.component.ts` — reusable 1-5 star input
170	- `confirm-dialog.component.ts` — policy confirmation modals
171	- `notification-bell.component.ts` — header inbox with badge
172	- `export-button.component.ts` — CSV/Excel download with confirmation toast
173	
174	### 3.3 Feature: Auth (`features/auth/`)
175	- `login.component.ts` — form with validation, lockout message display
176	- `login.component.html` — password rules hint, error states
177	
178	### 3.4 Feature: Client Workspace (`features/client/`)
179	- `client-dashboard.component.ts` — upcoming bookings, credit score summary
180	- `booking-create.component.ts` — form with lawyer/time selection, policy display
181	- `booking-list.component.ts` — status tracking, reschedule/cancel actions
182	- `booking-detail.component.ts` — full details, policy messages
183	- `credit-history.component.ts` — score + explainable changes
184	
185	### 3.5 Feature: Lawyer Workspace (`features/lawyer/`)
186	- `lawyer-dashboard.component.ts` — pending bookings, today's schedule
187	- `availability-manager.component.ts` — set/edit availability slots
188	- `booking-requests.component.ts` — accept/decline incoming bookings
189	
190	### 3.6 Feature: Admin Workspace (`features/admin/`)
191	- `admin-dashboard.component.ts` — summary cards (availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, efficiency)
192	- `dashboard-filters.component.ts` — org, date range, role filters
193	- `job-monitor.component.ts` — job list with state, latency, failure reasons
194	- `user-management.component.ts` — CRUD users
195	- `org-management.component.ts` — CRUD organizations
196	- `arbitration.component.ts` — dispute list, resolution form
197	
198	### 3.7 Feature: Reviews (`features/reviews/`)
199	- `review-form.component.ts` — 3 star categories + comment (1000 char counter)
200	- `review-list.component.ts` — past reviews given/received
201	- `dispute-form.component.ts` — file appeal (within 7 days)
202	
203	### 3.8 Feature: Reports (`features/reports/`)
204	- `report-viewer.component.ts` — dashboard visualizations
205	- `report-export.component.ts` — download with format selection + confirmation
206	- `subscription-manager.component.ts` — manage scheduled report subscriptions
207	
208	### 3.9 Feature: Notifications (`features/notifications/`)
209	- `notification-list.component.ts` — full notification inbox
210	- Integrated into `notification-bell.component.ts` in header
211	
212	### 3.10 Routing (`app.routes.ts`)
213	```
214	/login
215	/client/*          (guard: role=client)
216	/lawyer/*          (guard: role=lawyer)
217	/admin/*           (guard: role=admin)
218	/bookings/*        (guard: authenticated)
219	/reviews/*         (guard: authenticated)
220	/notifications     (guard: authenticated)
221	```
222	All feature routes lazy-loaded.
223	
224	---

228	| Step | What | Files |

# JusticeOps Offline Legal Services & Scheduling System — Implementation Plan

## Context
Build a full-stack offline-first legal services platform from scratch in an empty repo. Angular frontend + Fastify backend + PostgreSQL. The system coordinates legal casework, appointments, and compliance in air-gapped/low-connectivity environments with role-based access for Clients, Lawyers, and Administrators.

---

## Phase 1: Project Scaffolding & Database

### 1.1 Monorepo Setup
```
repo/
├── backend/                  # Fastify API
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app.ts           # Fastify app factory
│   │   ├── server.ts        # Entry point
│   │   ├── config/          # Environment & app config
│   │   ├── db/
│   │   │   ├── migrations/  # Knex migrations (numbered)
│   │   │   ├── seeds/       # Seed data
│   │   │   └── connection.ts
│   │   ├── plugins/         # Fastify plugins (auth, rate-limit, audit)
│   │   ├── modules/         # Feature modules (see below)
│   │   ├── middleware/       # Hooks, guards
│   │   ├── jobs/            # Job queue workers
│   │   └── utils/           # Encryption, masking, helpers
│   ├── knexfile.ts
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── frontend/                 # Angular app
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app/
│   │   │   ├── core/        # Guards, interceptors, services
│   │   │   ├── shared/      # Shared components, pipes, directives
│   │   │   ├── features/    # Feature modules by role
│   │   │   │   ├── auth/
│   │   │   │   ├── client/
│   │   │   │   ├── lawyer/
│   │   │   │   ├── admin/
│   │   │   │   ├── bookings/
│   │   │   │   ├── reviews/
│   │   │   │   ├── notifications/
│   │   │   │   └── reports/
│   │   │   ├── app.component.ts
│   │   │   ├── app.routes.ts
│   │   │   └── app.config.ts
│   │   └── environments/
│   ├── angular.json
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── docker-compose.yml        # PostgreSQL for local dev
└── README.md
```

### 1.2 Database Schema (Knex Migrations)

**Migration 001 — Core Tables:**
- `organizations` (id, name, settings_json, created_at)
- `users` (id, org_id FK, username, password_hash, role ENUM[client,lawyer,admin], credit_score DEFAULT 50, is_active, is_multi_tenant_admin, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, created_at, updated_at)
- `sessions` (id, user_id FK, token_jti, expires_at, created_at) — one active per user
- `idempotency_keys` (key, user_id, response_json, created_at, expires_at)

**Migration 002 — Booking Tables:**
- `availability` (id, lawyer_id FK, day_of_week, start_time, end_time, is_active)
- `bookings` (id, client_id FK, lawyer_id FK, org_id FK, type ENUM[consultation,milestone], status ENUM[pending,confirmed,completed,cancelled,no_show,rescheduled], scheduled_at, completed_at, cancellation_reason, idempotency_key, created_at, updated_at)

**Migration 003 — Reviews & Trust:**
- `reviews` (id, booking_id FK, reviewer_id FK, reviewee_id FK, timeliness_rating 1-5, professionalism_rating 1-5, communication_rating 1-5, comment VARCHAR(1000), created_at)
- `disputes` (id, review_id FK, appellant_id FK, reason, status ENUM[open,under_review,resolved,dismissed], resolution_notes, filed_at, resolved_at) — 7-day appeal window
- `credit_score_history` (id, user_id FK, change_amount, reason, rule_code, new_score, created_at)

**Migration 004 — Jobs & Notifications:**
- `jobs` (id, type, payload_json, priority, status ENUM[queued,running,completed,failed,dead], attempts, max_attempts DEFAULT 5, next_retry_at, last_error, shard_key, idempotency_key, created_at, started_at, completed_at)
- `notifications` (id, user_id FK, title, body, is_read DEFAULT false, created_at)
- `report_subscriptions` (id, user_id FK, report_type, filters_json, schedule_cron DEFAULT '0 8 * * *', is_active, created_at)

**Migration 005 — Security & Audit:**
- `audit_log` (id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, details_json, ip_address, created_at) — immutable, no UPDATE/DELETE
- `webhook_configs` (id, org_id FK, url, events_json, secret, is_active, created_at)
- `config_dictionaries` (id, org_id, category, key, value_json, created_at)
- `workflow_steps` (id, org_id, workflow_type, step_order, name, config_json)

**Sensitive field encryption:** `bookings.cancellation_reason`, user notes, identifiers — AES-256 via utility, key from env var.

### 1.3 Key Backend Packages
`fastify`, `@fastify/jwt`, `@fastify/cors`, `@fastify/rate-limit`, `knex`, `pg`, `bcrypt`, `uuid`, `node-cron`, `exceljs`, `json2csv`, `crypto` (built-in for AES-256)

---

## Phase 2: Backend API Modules

### 2.1 Authentication & Authorization (`modules/auth/`)
- **POST /api/auth/login** — validate password (bcrypt), check lockout (5 fails → 15min lock), issue JWT with jti, invalidate prior session, return token + role + menu permissions
- **POST /api/auth/logout** — invalidate session
- **Password validation** — 12+ chars, 1 number, 1 symbol
- **Middleware:** `authenticate` hook (verify JWT + session exists), `authorize(roles[])` hook, `orgIsolation` hook
- **Admin exempt config** for single-session rule

### 2.2 Users & Organizations (`modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/`)
- CRUD users (admin only), org-scoped queries
- GET /api/users/me — profile + credit score
- Org isolation enforced at query level (WHERE org_id = ?)

### 2.3 Bookings (`modules/bookings/`)
- **POST /api/bookings** — create with idempotency key, advisory lock on lawyer+timeslot (`pg_advisory_xact_lock`), rate-limit (20/min/user, 200/min/org)
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/reschedule** — with policy check (2h window)
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/cancel** — apply credit penalty if within 2h
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/confirm** — lawyer accepts
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/decline** — lawyer declines
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/complete** — triggers review prompt
- **PATCH /api/bookings/:id/no-show** — apply -10 credit, 10min grace period
- **GET /api/bookings** — filtered by role, status, date range

### 2.4 Availability (`modules/availability/`)
- CRUD for lawyer availability slots
- GET /api/availability/:lawyerId — public for booking

### 2.5 Reviews & Disputes (`modules/reviews/`)
- **POST /api/reviews** — 1-5 stars x3 categories + comment (1000 char max)
- **POST /api/disputes** — file within 7 days of review
- **PATCH /api/disputes/:id** — admin arbitration resolution
- **GET /api/disputes** — admin view with filters

### 2.6 Credit Scores (`modules/credit/`)
- GET /api/credit/:userId — score + history
- Internal service: `applyRule(userId, ruleCode)` — updates score, logs to history
- Configurable rules in `config_dictionaries`

### 2.7 Reports & Export (`modules/reports/`)
- **GET /api/reports/dashboard** — availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, closed-loop efficiency
- **GET /api/reports/export?format=csv|xlsx** — generate and return file
- **CRUD /api/report-subscriptions** — manage scheduled reports

### 2.8 Notifications (`modules/notifications/`)
- **GET /api/notifications** — paginated, unread count
- **PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read**
- Internal: `createNotification(userId, title, body)`

### 2.9 Jobs (`modules/jobs/`)
- **GET /api/jobs** — admin view with state/latency/failure filters
- **Worker process:** polls `jobs` table, claims with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, exponential backoff (attempt^2 * 1000ms), max 5 retries, idempotency guard
- **Job types:** report-generation, record-import, compliance-check
- **Cron:** node-cron runs at 8:00 AM → creates report-generation jobs for active subscriptions → on completion, inserts notification

### 2.10 Webhooks (`modules/webhooks/`)
- Internal dispatcher: on key events (booking created, completed, etc.), POST to configured org webhook URLs
- Fire-and-forget with retry (3 attempts), log results to audit

### 2.11 Audit & Security
- **Audit plugin:** Fastify onResponse hook logs all mutating requests to `audit_log`
- **Encryption util:** AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt for sensitive fields, key from `ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var
- **Log masking:** custom serializer strips PII from request/response logs

---

## Phase 3: Frontend (Angular)

### 3.1 Core (`core/`)
- `auth.service.ts` — login, logout, token storage, role getter
- `auth.interceptor.ts` — attach JWT to requests
- `error.interceptor.ts` — handle 401 (redirect login), 403 (permission toast), 429 (rate limit feedback)
- `auth.guard.ts` — route guard checking role
- `notification.service.ts` — poll GET /api/notifications on interval
- `idempotency.service.ts` — generate UUID keys for booking requests

### 3.2 Shared (`shared/`)
- `role-menu.component.ts` — dynamic navigation based on user role
- `permission-feedback.component.ts` — inline "You don't have permission" messages
- `star-rating.component.ts` — reusable 1-5 star input
- `confirm-dialog.component.ts` — policy confirmation modals
- `notification-bell.component.ts` — header inbox with badge
- `export-button.component.ts` — CSV/Excel download with confirmation toast

### 3.3 Feature: Auth (`features/auth/`)
- `login.component.ts` — form with validation, lockout message display
- `login.component.html` — password rules hint, error states

### 3.4 Feature: Client Workspace (`features/client/`)
- `client-dashboard.component.ts` — upcoming bookings, credit score summary
- `booking-create.component.ts` — form with lawyer/time selection, policy display
- `booking-list.component.ts` — status tracking, reschedule/cancel actions
- `booking-detail.component.ts` — full details, policy messages
- `credit-history.component.ts` — score + explainable changes

### 3.5 Feature: Lawyer Workspace (`features/lawyer/`)
- `lawyer-dashboard.component.ts` — pending bookings, today's schedule
- `availability-manager.component.ts` — set/edit availability slots
- `booking-requests.component.ts` — accept/decline incoming bookings

### 3.6 Feature: Admin Workspace (`features/admin/`)
- `admin-dashboard.component.ts` — summary cards (availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, efficiency)
- `dashboard-filters.component.ts` — org, date range, role filters
- `job-monitor.component.ts` — job list with state, latency, failure reasons
- `user-management.component.ts` — CRUD users
- `org-management.component.ts` — CRUD organizations
- `arbitration.component.ts` — dispute list, resolution form

### 3.7 Feature: Reviews (`features/reviews/`)
- `review-form.component.ts` — 3 star categories + comment (1000 char counter)
- `review-list.component.ts` — past reviews given/received
- `dispute-form.component.ts` — file appeal (within 7 days)

### 3.8 Feature: Reports (`features/reports/`)
- `report-viewer.component.ts` — dashboard visualizations
- `report-export.component.ts` — download with format selection + confirmation
- `subscription-manager.component.ts` — manage scheduled report subscriptions

### 3.9 Feature: Notifications (`features/notifications/`)
- `notification-list.component.ts` — full notification inbox
- Integrated into `notification-bell.component.ts` in header

### 3.10 Routing (`app.routes.ts`)
```
/login
/client/*          (guard: role=client)
/lawyer/*          (guard: role=lawyer)
/admin/*           (guard: role=admin)
/bookings/*        (guard: authenticated)
/reviews/*         (guard: authenticated)
/notifications     (guard: authenticated)
```
All feature routes lazy-loaded.

---

## Phase 4: Implementation Order

| Step | What | Files |
|------|------|-------|
| 1 | Init monorepo, package.json, tsconfig, docker-compose | Root configs |
| 2 | Backend scaffolding: Fastify app, config, DB connection | `backend/src/app.ts`, `server.ts`, `config/`, `db/` |
| 3 | DB migrations (all 5) | `backend/src/db/migrations/` |
| 4 | Auth module (login, JWT, session, lockout, password rules) | `backend/src/modules/auth/` |
| 5 | Auth middleware (authenticate, authorize, orgIsolation) | `backend/src/plugins/`, `backend/src/middleware/` |
| 6 | Users & Organizations CRUD | `backend/src/modules/users/`, `modules/organizations/` |
| 7 | Availability & Bookings (with advisory locks, rate limiting, idempotency) | `backend/src/modules/availability/`, `modules/bookings/` |
| 8 | Reviews, Disputes, Credit Scores | `backend/src/modules/reviews/`, `modules/credit/` |
| 9 | Jobs queue, worker, cron scheduler | `backend/src/jobs/`, `modules/jobs/` |
| 10 | Reports, export, subscriptions | `backend/src/modules/reports/` |
| 11 | Notifications | `backend/src/modules/notifications/` |
| 12 | Webhooks, audit plugin, encryption util | `backend/src/modules/webhooks/`, `plugins/audit.ts`, `utils/` |
| 13 | Angular scaffolding, core services, interceptors, guards | `frontend/src/app/core/` |
| 14 | Shared components (menu, star-rating, dialogs, notification bell) | `frontend/src/app/shared/` |
| 15 | Auth feature (login page) | `frontend/src/app/features/auth/` |
| 16 | Client workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/client/` |
| 17 | Lawyer workspace | `frontend/src/app/features/lawyer/` |
| 18 | Admin workspace (dashboards, job monitor, arbitration) | `frontend/src/app/features/admin/` |
| 19 | Reviews & Reports features | `frontend/src/app/features/reviews/`, `features/reports/` |
| 20 | Notifications feature | `frontend/src/app/features/notifications/` |
| 21 | Seed data & end-to-end smoke test | Seeds, manual testing |

---

## Verification
1. `docker-compose up` — PostgreSQL running
2. `cd backend && npm run migrate && npm run dev` — API on :3000
3. `cd frontend && ng serve` — UI on :4200
4. Create admin user via seed → login → verify JWT + session
5. Create lawyer + client → test booking flow with concurrency (two simultaneous requests)
6. Complete booking → verify review prompts appear for both parties
7. File dispute → verify admin arbitration view
8. Check admin dashboard metrics populate
9. Create report subscription → verify 8AM job creates notification
10. Export report → verify CSV/Excel downloads with confirmation
