# JusticeOps Offline Legal Services & Scheduling System — Implementation Plan

## Context
Build a full-stack offline-first legal coordination platform from scratch (empty repo). Designed for air-gapped/LAN-only environments — no external APIs, Cloud KMS, CDN, or public NTP. Angular frontend + Fastify backend + PostgreSQL (sole persistence, queue, and audit engine).

---

## Phase 1: Project Scaffolding & Database

### 1.1 Monorepo Structure
```
repo/
├── backend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app.ts                  # Fastify app factory
│   │   ├── server.ts               # Entry point
│   │   ├── config/
│   │   │   ├── index.ts            # Env-based config (DB, JWT secret, encryption key, rate limits)
│   │   │   └── credit-rules.ts     # Default credit score rules (overridable via config_dictionaries)
│   │   ├── db/
│   │   │   ├── migrations/         # Knex numbered migrations
│   │   │   ├── seeds/              # Dev seed data
│   │   │   └── connection.ts       # Knex instance
│   │   ├── plugins/
│   │   │   ├── auth.plugin.ts      # JWT verify + session_nonce validation
│   │   │   ├── authorize.plugin.ts # Role-based route decorator
│   │   │   ├── org-isolation.plugin.ts  # Inject org_id into all queries
│   │   │   ├── rate-limit.plugin.ts     # Token-bucket from rate_limit_buckets table
│   │   │   ├── audit.plugin.ts     # onResponse hook → audit_log
│   │   │   └── time-sync.plugin.ts # GET /api/time endpoint for client offset
│   │   ├── modules/
│   │   │   ├── auth/               # Login, logout, password validation, lockout
│   │   │   ├── users/              # User CRUD, profile
│   │   │   ├── organizations/      # Org CRUD, settings
│   │   │   ├── availability/       # Lawyer schedule management
│   │   │   ├── bookings/           # Booking lifecycle + advisory locks
│   │   │   ├── reviews/            # Reviews + disputes + arbitration
│   │   │   ├── credit/             # Credit score engine + history
│   │   │   ├── jobs/               # Job admin API (GET list, state monitoring)
│   │   │   ├── reports/            # Dashboard metrics, export, subscriptions
│   │   │   ├── notifications/      # In-app notification CRUD
│   │   │   └── webhooks/           # Local webhook dispatch
│   │   ├── workers/
│   │   │   ├── job-worker.ts       # FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED poller
│   │   │   ├── scheduler.ts        # node-cron: 8AM report gen (with jitter), 2AM vacuum
│   │   │   └── handlers/           # report-generation.ts, record-import.ts, compliance-check.ts, vacuum.ts
│   │   └── utils/
│   │       ├── encryption.ts       # AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt + pre-save hooks
│   │       ├── log-masker.ts       # PII redaction serializer for stdout
│   │       ├── password.ts         # Validation rules + bcrypt hashing
│   │       └── idempotency.ts      # Check/store idempotency keys
│   ├── knexfile.ts
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── frontend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── app/
│   │   │   ├── core/
│   │   │   │   ├── services/
│   │   │   │   │   ├── auth.service.ts
│   │   │   │   │   ├── time-sync.service.ts    # Server time offset
│   │   │   │   │   ├── notification.service.ts
│   │   │   │   │   └── idempotency.service.ts
│   │   │   │   ├── interceptors/
│   │   │   │   │   ├── auth.interceptor.ts
│   │   │   │   │   └── error.interceptor.ts
│   │   │   │   └── guards/
│   │   │   │       ├── auth.guard.ts
│   │   │   │       └── role.guard.ts
│   │   │   ├── shared/
│   │   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   │   ├── role-menu/
│   │   │   │   │   ├── permission-feedback/
│   │   │   │   │   ├── star-rating/
│   │   │   │   │   ├── confirm-dialog/
│   │   │   │   │   ├── notification-bell/
│   │   │   │   │   └── export-button/
│   │   │   │   └── pipes/
│   │   │   ├── features/
│   │   │   │   ├── auth/
│   │   │   │   ├── client/
│   │   │   │   ├── lawyer/
│   │   │   │   ├── admin/
│   │   │   │   ├── reviews/
│   │   │   │   ├── reports/
│   │   │   │   └── notifications/
│   │   │   ├── app.component.ts
│   │   │   ├── app.routes.ts
│   │   │   └── app.config.ts
│   │   └── environments/
│   ├── angular.json
│   ├── package.json
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── docker-compose.yml          # PostgreSQL 16
└── README.md
```

### 1.2 Database Schema (Knex Migrations)

**Migration 001 — Organizations & Users:**
```sql
organizations (id UUID PK, name, settings JSONB, created_at, updated_at)

users (
  id UUID PK, org_id FK→organizations, username UNIQUE, password_hash,
  role ENUM('client','lawyer','admin','super_admin'),
  credit_score INT DEFAULT 50 CHECK(0..100),
  is_active BOOL, is_session_exempt BOOL DEFAULT false,
  failed_login_attempts INT DEFAULT 0, locked_until TIMESTAMPTZ,
  daily_capacity INT,          -- lawyers only: max workload weight per day
  created_at, updated_at
)
```

**Migration 002 — Sessions & Idempotency:**
```sql
user_sessions (
  id UUID PK, user_id FK→users, session_nonce VARCHAR UNIQUE,
  token_jti VARCHAR, ip_address, workstation_id,
  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at
)
-- Unique index on (user_id) WHERE is_session_exempt=false enforces one session

idempotency_registry (
  key VARCHAR PK, user_id FK, method VARCHAR, path VARCHAR,
  status_code INT, response_body JSONB,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)

rate_limit_buckets (
  id VARCHAR PK,               -- e.g. "user:{id}" or "org:{id}"
  tokens FLOAT, max_tokens FLOAT, refill_rate FLOAT,
  last_refill_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
```

**Migration 003 — Bookings & Availability:**
```sql
availability (
  id UUID PK, lawyer_id FK→users, day_of_week INT(0-6),
  start_time TIME, end_time TIME, slot_duration_min INT DEFAULT 60,
  is_active BOOL, created_at
)

bookings (
  id UUID PK, client_id FK→users, lawyer_id FK→users, org_id FK→organizations,
  type ENUM('consultation','milestone'),
  status ENUM('pending','confirmed','completed','cancelled','no_show','rescheduled','declined'),
  scheduled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,     -- consultations: exact time
  deadline_at TIMESTAMPTZ,      -- milestones: due date
  weight INT DEFAULT 1,         -- milestones: capacity weight consumed
  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, cancellation_reason_enc BYTEA,
  idempotency_key VARCHAR, created_at, updated_at
)
```

**Migration 004 — Reviews, Disputes & Credit:**
```sql
reviews (
  id UUID PK, booking_id FK→bookings, reviewer_id FK, reviewee_id FK,
  timeliness INT CHECK(1..5), professionalism INT CHECK(1..5),
  communication INT CHECK(1..5), comment VARCHAR(1000),
  created_at
)

disputes (
  id UUID PK, review_id FK→reviews, appellant_id FK,
  reason TEXT, status ENUM('pending','under_review','resolved','dismissed'),
  resolution_notes TEXT, admin_id FK,
  penalty_escrowed JSONB,       -- snapshot of score changes held in escrow
  filed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deadline_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
-- CHECK: filed_at <= review.created_at + INTERVAL '7 days'

credit_score_history (
  id UUID PK, user_id FK, previous_score INT, change_amount INT,
  new_score INT, rule_code VARCHAR, reason TEXT,
  is_escrowed BOOL DEFAULT false, dispute_id FK NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
```

**Migration 005 — Jobs & Notifications:**
```sql
jobs (
  id UUID PK, type VARCHAR, payload JSONB, priority INT DEFAULT 0,
  status ENUM('queued','running','completed','failed','dead'),
  attempts INT DEFAULT 0, max_attempts INT DEFAULT 5,
  next_retry_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_error TEXT,
  shard_key INT, idempotency_key VARCHAR,
  locked_by VARCHAR,            -- worker node ID
  created_at, started_at, completed_at
)
-- Index on (status, priority, next_retry_at) for efficient polling

notifications (
  id UUID PK, user_id FK, title VARCHAR, body TEXT,
  type VARCHAR, reference_id UUID,
  is_read BOOL DEFAULT false, created_at
)

report_subscriptions (
  id UUID PK, user_id FK, report_type VARCHAR,
  filters JSONB, is_active BOOL DEFAULT true, created_at
)
```

**Migration 006 — Audit, Webhooks & Config:**
```sql
audit_log (
  id BIGSERIAL PK, user_id UUID, action VARCHAR,
  entity_type VARCHAR, entity_id UUID,
  old_state JSONB, new_state JSONB,    -- full unmasked OLD/NEW
  ip_address VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
-- REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE on audit_log (immutable)
-- Access restricted to super_admin role via RLS

webhook_configs (
  id UUID PK, org_id FK, url VARCHAR, events JSONB,
  secret VARCHAR, is_active BOOL, created_at
)

config_dictionaries (
  id UUID PK, org_id FK, category VARCHAR, key VARCHAR,
  value JSONB, created_at
)

workflow_steps (
  id UUID PK, org_id FK, workflow_type VARCHAR,
  step_order INT, name VARCHAR, config JSONB
)
```

**Migration 007 — Row-Level Security:**
```sql
-- Enable RLS on: users, bookings, availability, reviews, notifications
-- Policies: current_setting('app.current_org_id') = org_id
-- Super admin bypasses RLS
-- Set via: SET LOCAL app.current_org_id = ?  (in transaction)
```

**Migration 008 — Paper Backup Tracking:**
```sql
system_config (
  key VARCHAR PK, value JSONB, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ
)
-- Key: 'encryption_key_backup_confirmed' → {confirmed: bool, confirmed_by: uuid, confirmed_at: timestamp}
```

### 1.3 Key Backend Packages
`fastify`, `@fastify/jwt`, `@fastify/cors`, `knex`, `pg`, `bcrypt`, `uuid`, `node-cron`, `exceljs`, `json2csv`, `pino` (logging with custom serializers)

No `@fastify/rate-limit` — custom token-bucket plugin reading from `rate_limit_buckets` table for cross-node sync.

---

## Phase 2: Backend Core Infrastructure

### 2.1 Authentication (`modules/auth/`)
- **POST /api/auth/login**
  1. Find user by username
  2. Check `locked_until` > now → return 423 with remaining lockout time
  3. Compare bcrypt hash → on fail: increment `failed_login_attempts`, set `locked_until` if attempts >= 5
  4. On success: reset attempts, generate JWT with `jti` + `session_nonce`
  5. Delete existing row in `user_sessions` for this user (unless `is_session_exempt`)
  6. Insert new `user_sessions` row with nonce, jti, workstation info
  7. Return token + user profile + role-based menu permissions
- **POST /api/auth/logout** — delete session row, return 204
- **GET /api/auth/me** — current user profile

### 2.2 Session Nonce Validation (`plugins/auth.plugin.ts`)
Every request:
1. Decode JWT → extract `jti` and `session_nonce`
2. Query `user_sessions` WHERE `user_id` AND `session_nonce` matches AND not expired
3. If no match → 401 "Session Revoked" (another workstation logged in)

### 2.3 Org Isolation (`plugins/org-isolation.plugin.ts`)
- On each authenticated request: `SET LOCAL app.current_org_id = :orgId` within the request's transaction
- PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce filtering automatically
- Super admins: `SET LOCAL app.current_org_id = ''` (bypasses RLS)

### 2.4 Rate Limiting (`plugins/rate-limit.plugin.ts`)
- Token-bucket persisted in `rate_limit_buckets` table
- On each booking write: atomic UPDATE to consume token, check remaining
- Refill tokens based on elapsed time since `last_refill_at`
- Limits: 20 writes/min/user, 200 writes/min/org
- Returns 429 with `Retry-After` header when exhausted

### 2.5 Time Sync (`plugins/time-sync.plugin.ts`)
- **GET /api/time** — returns `{ server_time: ISO8601 }` (no auth required)
- Frontend calls on app init and periodically to compute offset
- All policy checks (no-show 10min, cancel 2h) use server time exclusively

### 2.6 Encryption (`utils/encryption.ts`)
- AES-256-GCM using `ENCRYPTION_KEY` from env var
- `encrypt(plaintext)` → `iv:authTag:ciphertext` (base64)
- `decrypt(blob)` → plaintext
- Pre-save hooks in booking/notes modules call encrypt before INSERT/UPDATE
- **Paper Backup Flow:** On first server start, if `system_config['encryption_key_backup_confirmed']` is false, API returns 503 for all non-admin routes. Admin must POST /api/admin/confirm-key-backup to acknowledge.

### 2.7 Audit (`plugins/audit.plugin.ts`)
- Fastify `onResponse` hook for all mutating methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE)
- Logs: user_id, action, entity, old_state (from pre-hook), new_state, IP
- `audit_log` table: REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE at migration level
- **Stdout logs:** `log-masker.ts` Pino serializer redacts PII patterns (names, phones, emails)
- **Audit JSONB:** stores full unmasked state, accessible only to super_admin via RLS

---

## Phase 3: Backend Feature Modules

### 3.1 Bookings (`modules/bookings/`)

**Consultations (time-blocked):**
- POST /api/bookings — with idempotency key
  1. Check `idempotency_registry` → return cached response if exists
  2. Check user credit_score >= threshold (default 20) → 403 "Account Under Review" if below
  3. Rate limit check (token bucket)
  4. BEGIN transaction
  5. `SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(lawyer_id || scheduled_at))` — lock the slot
  6. Check no conflicting confirmed booking for same lawyer+time
  7. INSERT booking
  8. Store response in `idempotency_registry` (expires 24h)
  9. COMMIT
- Policy enforcement uses server time exclusively

**Milestones (capacity-based):**
- POST /api/bookings (type=milestone)
  1. Same idempotency + rate limit + credit checks
  2. BEGIN transaction
  3. Sum existing milestone weights for lawyer on deadline_at date
  4. Check `total_weight + new_weight <= lawyer.daily_capacity`
  5. INSERT booking with weight
  6. COMMIT
- No time-slot lock needed; uses capacity math instead

**Status transitions:**
- PATCH /api/bookings/:id/reschedule — check 2h window (server time), re-run slot/capacity check
- PATCH /api/bookings/:id/cancel — if within 2h: apply credit penalty (-5), record in credit_score_history
- PATCH /api/bookings/:id/confirm — lawyer accepts
- PATCH /api/bookings/:id/decline — lawyer declines
- PATCH /api/bookings/:id/complete — triggers notification to both parties for review
- PATCH /api/bookings/:id/no-show — 10min grace (server time), apply -10 credit
- GET /api/bookings — filtered by role/status/date, org-isolated

### 3.2 Reviews & Disputes (`modules/reviews/`)
- POST /api/reviews — validates booking is completed, both parties haven't already reviewed
- POST /api/disputes — validates within 7 days of review `created_at`
  - Moves associated credit_score_history entries to `is_escrowed = true`
  - Sets dispute status to `pending`
  - Score change is reversed (held in escrow) until admin rules
- PATCH /api/disputes/:id/resolve — admin only
  - `status` → resolved or dismissed
  - If dismissed: re-apply escrowed penalty
  - If resolved: discard escrowed penalty (score stays restored)

### 3.3 Credit Score Engine (`modules/credit/`)
- `applyRule(userId, ruleCode, context)`:
  1. Look up rule in `config_dictionaries` (or use defaults from `credit-rules.ts`)
  2. Calculate change amount
  3. Clamp to 0-100
  4. UPDATE users SET credit_score
  5. INSERT credit_score_history with explainable reason
- **Throttle check:** if `credit_score < threshold` → disable bookings
- **Streak tracking:** query last 5 bookings; if all on-time → apply +2 bonus
- GET /api/credit/:userId — score + paginated history (authorized staff or self)

### 3.4 Jobs & Workers (`workers/`)

**Transactional Outbox Pattern:**
- Modules insert into `jobs` table within their business transaction
- No external broker (no Redis/RabbitMQ)

**Job Worker (`job-worker.ts`):**
```
loop every 5s:
  BEGIN
  SELECT * FROM jobs
    WHERE status IN ('queued','failed') AND attempts < max_attempts
      AND (next_retry_at IS NULL OR next_retry_at <= now())
    ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC
    FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
    LIMIT 5
  UPDATE status='running', locked_by=NODE_ID, started_at=now()
  COMMIT

  for each job:
    try: execute handler(job)
    on success: UPDATE status='completed', completed_at=now()
    on failure:
      attempts++
      if attempts >= max_attempts: status='dead'
      else: status='failed', next_retry_at = now() + (2^attempts * 1000ms)
      last_error = error.message
```

**Scheduler (`scheduler.ts` via node-cron):**
- **8:00 AM daily — Report subscription notifications:**
  - Query active `report_subscriptions`
  - For each: insert `jobs` row with type='report-generation'
  - **Jitter:** Each job gets `scheduled_at = 8:00 + random(0..15min)` to stagger DB load
  - On job completion: insert notification into `notifications` table

- **2:00 AM daily — Idempotency vacuum:**
  - Insert job with type='idempotency-vacuum'
  - Handler: `DELETE FROM idempotency_registry WHERE expires_at < now()`

**Job Handlers (`workers/handlers/`):**
- `report-generation.ts` — query dashboard metrics per subscription filters, generate payload, create notification
- `record-import.ts` — incremental crawl/import of records
- `compliance-check.ts` — scan bookings for policy violations
- `vacuum.ts` — purge expired idempotency keys

### 3.5 Reports & Export (`modules/reports/`)
- **GET /api/reports/dashboard** — computes:
  - Availability: % of lawyer slots filled vs total
  - Fault rate: no-shows + cancellations / total bookings
  - Utilization: completed bookings / available capacity
  - Throughput volume: bookings completed per period
  - Closed-loop efficiency: alerts resolved / alerts created
- Filters: org_id, date_range, role
- **GET /api/reports/export?format=csv|xlsx** — streams file response
- **CRUD /api/report-subscriptions** — manage what reports generate at 8AM

### 3.6 Notifications (`modules/notifications/`)
- GET /api/notifications?unread=true — paginated, includes unread count in response header
- PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read — mark as read
- Internal `createNotification(userId, title, body, type, referenceId)`

### 3.7 Webhooks (`modules/webhooks/`)
- On key events (booking.created, booking.completed, dispute.filed, etc.)
- Query `webhook_configs` for org where event matches
- POST to local/on-prem URL with HMAC signature
- Fire with retry (3 attempts, 5s backoff), log to audit

---

## Phase 4: Frontend (Angular)

### 4.1 Core Services
- **`auth.service.ts`** — login/logout, store JWT in localStorage, expose role/user observables
- **`time-sync.service.ts`** — call GET /api/time on init + every 5min, compute `serverOffset = serverTime - clientTime`, expose `serverNow()` method. All UI time displays and policy checks use this.
- **`notification.service.ts`** — poll /api/notifications every 30s, expose unread count observable
- **`idempotency.service.ts`** — generate UUID v4 keys for booking mutations
- **`auth.interceptor.ts`** — attach `Authorization: Bearer <token>` to all API calls
- **`error.interceptor.ts`** — 401 → redirect to login (session revoked message), 403 → show permission feedback component, 423 → show lockout timer, 429 → show rate limit message

### 4.2 Shared Components
- **`role-menu/`** — sidebar/top nav rendered from role-based menu config returned by login
- **`permission-feedback/`** — inline banner: "Action Restricted: Requires Admin Role" (shown when API returns 403 or preemptively based on role)
- **`star-rating/`** — reusable 1-5 star input/display
- **`confirm-dialog/`** — modal for policy confirmations (cancel penalty, no-show warning)
- **`notification-bell/`** — header icon with unread badge, dropdown list
- **`export-button/`** — triggers download, shows "File saved successfully" toast

### 4.3 Auth Feature (`features/auth/`)
- **Login page:** username/password form, password rules hint (12+ chars, 1 number, 1 symbol), lockout countdown display, "Session revoked" alert if redirected from 401

### 4.4 Client Workspace (`features/client/`)
- **Dashboard:** upcoming bookings, credit score gauge, policy banners ("no-show after 10 min = violation", "cancel within 2h = credit penalty")
- **Booking create:** lawyer selection, available slot picker (consultations) or deadline picker (milestones), policy confirmation dialog, idempotency key attached
- **Booking list:** filterable by status, reschedule/cancel actions with policy warnings
- **Credit history:** score display + timeline of changes with explanations
- **Booking disabled state:** if credit < 20, "New Booking" button disabled with "Account Under Review" message

### 4.5 Lawyer Workspace (`features/lawyer/`)
- **Dashboard:** today's schedule, pending requests count, workload capacity gauge (used/total for milestones)
- **Availability manager:** set recurring weekly slots (day, start, end, duration)
- **Booking requests:** accept/decline with notes, view client credit score
- **Workload view:** daily capacity usage showing milestone weights

### 4.6 Admin Workspace (`features/admin/`)
- **Operational dashboard:** summary cards for availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, closed-loop efficiency
- **Filters:** organization dropdown, date range picker, role selector
- **Job monitor:** table with status, type, attempts, latency, last_error; color-coded status badges
- **Arbitration:** dispute list with pending/resolved tabs, resolution form (resolve or dismiss + notes)
- **User management:** CRUD with role assignment, session exempt toggle
- **Org management:** CRUD organizations, settings
- **Paper backup confirmation:** on first admin login if not confirmed, modal prompting key backup acknowledgment

### 4.7 Reviews (`features/reviews/`)
- **Review form:** appears after booking completion, 3 star categories + comment textarea (1000 char counter)
- **Review list:** given/received tabs with averages
- **Dispute form:** appeal within 7 days, reason textarea

### 4.8 Reports (`features/reports/`)
- **Report viewer:** charts (bar/line) for dashboard metrics
- **Export:** format selector (CSV/Excel) + download trigger + "Saved to disk" confirmation toast
- **Subscriptions:** manage report subscriptions (type, filters, active toggle)

### 4.9 Notifications (`features/notifications/`)
- **Full inbox page:** list with read/unread, type icons, timestamps, click to navigate to reference
- **Bell component** in header: badge with count, dropdown preview

### 4.10 Routing
```
/login                          (public)
/client/dashboard               (guard: client)
/client/bookings                (guard: client)
/client/bookings/new            (guard: client)
/client/credit                  (guard: client)
/lawyer/dashboard               (guard: lawyer)
/lawyer/availability            (guard: lawyer)
/lawyer/bookings                (guard: lawyer)
/admin/dashboard                (guard: admin|super_admin)
/admin/jobs                     (guard: admin|super_admin)
/admin/arbitration              (guard: admin|super_admin)
/admin/users                    (guard: admin|super_admin)
/admin/organizations            (guard: super_admin)
/reviews                        (guard: authenticated)
/reports                        (guard: admin|super_admin)
/reports/subscriptions          (guard: admin|super_admin)
/notifications                  (guard: authenticated)
```
All feature routes lazy-loaded via `loadChildren`.

---

## Phase 5: Implementation Order

| Step | What | Key Details |
|------|------|-------------|
| 1 | Monorepo init | package.json (root + backend + frontend), tsconfig, docker-compose (PG 16), .env.example |
| 2 | Backend scaffold | Fastify app factory, config loader, Knex connection, Pino logger with masker |
| 3 | DB migrations 001-008 | All tables, RLS policies, indexes, constraints, REVOKE on audit_log |
| 4 | Auth module + session nonce | Login/logout, bcrypt, JWT, session_nonce, lockout, password rules |
| 5 | Core plugins | auth.plugin (nonce validation), org-isolation (RLS SET LOCAL), rate-limit (token bucket), time-sync, audit |
| 6 | Encryption util + paper backup | AES-256-GCM, pre-save hooks, system_config check, admin confirmation endpoint |
| 7 | Users & Organizations | CRUD, org-scoped, super_admin multi-tenant |
| 8 | Availability | Lawyer schedule CRUD |
| 9 | Bookings | Consultations (advisory lock) + Milestones (capacity check), idempotency, all status transitions, policy enforcement |
| 10 | Credit score engine | applyRule, streak tracking, threshold gating, history |
| 11 | Reviews & Disputes | Two-way reviews, dispute filing, escrow logic, admin arbitration |
| 12 | Jobs & Workers | Transactional outbox, FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED worker, exponential backoff, handlers |
| 13 | Scheduler | node-cron: 8AM report gen with 15min jitter, 2AM vacuum |
| 14 | Reports & Export | Dashboard metrics computation, CSV/Excel streaming, subscriptions |
| 15 | Notifications | CRUD, integration with job completion |
| 16 | Webhooks | Local dispatch with HMAC, retry, audit logging |
| 17 | Seed data | Default org, admin, lawyer, client users; sample availability + bookings |
| 18 | Angular scaffold | ng new, standalone config, routing, environments |
| 19 | Core services & interceptors | auth, time-sync, notification, idempotency services; auth + error interceptors; guards |
| 20 | Shared components | role-menu, permission-feedback, star-rating, confirm-dialog, notification-bell, export-button |
| 21 | Auth feature | Login page with all states |
| 22 | Client workspace | Dashboard, booking create/list, credit history |
| 23 | Lawyer workspace | Dashboard, availability manager, booking requests, workload view |
| 24 | Admin workspace | Dashboard, filters, job monitor, arbitration, user/org management, paper backup modal |
| 25 | Reviews + Reports features | Review form/list, dispute form, report viewer, export, subscriptions |
| 26 | Notifications feature | Inbox page, bell integration |
| 27 | Integration testing | End-to-end smoke tests across all flows |

---

## Verification
1. `docker-compose up -d` — PostgreSQL running
2. `cd backend && npm run migrate && npm run seed && npm run dev` — API on :3000
3. Paper backup confirmation — verify admin must confirm before system is usable
4. `cd frontend && ng serve` — UI on :4200
5. Login as admin → verify JWT + session nonce → login again from "different workstation" → verify first session revoked
6. Test lockout: 5 wrong passwords → verify 15min lock with countdown
7. Time sync: verify /api/time returns server time, frontend computes offset
8. Create lawyer availability → create client booking (consultation) → verify advisory lock prevents double-book
9. Create milestone booking → verify capacity weight is checked
10. Rate limit: fire 21 rapid booking requests → verify 429 on 21st
11. Complete booking → verify both parties prompted for review
12. Submit review → file dispute within 7 days → verify score escrowed → admin resolves
13. Credit score < 20 → verify booking disabled in UI + 403 from API
14. Check admin dashboard metrics with org/date/role filters
15. Create report subscription → verify 8AM job created with jitter → notification appears
16. 2AM vacuum → verify expired idempotency keys purged
17. Export report → verify CSV/Excel file downloads with confirmation toast
18. Audit log → verify immutable, full OLD/NEW states, super_admin access only
19. Stdout logs → verify PII is masked

content.text:
The plan has been completely rewritten. Here's what changed from the previous version:

**Architecture:** Switched from flat module structure to **Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters)** — domain logic is framework-free and testable in isolation. Business rules live in `domain/`, use cases in `application/`, Fastify/Knex in `infrastructure/` and `api/`.

**Docker-First:** `docker compose up --build` is the canonical start command. `run_tests.sh` is the unified test entry. Auto-migration + auto-seed on container start. No manual steps.

**18 Vertical Slice Modules** — each with defined:
- Responsibilities, flows, inputs/outputs
- Specific failure paths (401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 423, 429)
- Permission boundaries
- Tests that must exist (unit, integration, E2E)
- Completion criteria checklist

**Testing:** 90%+ coverage target. TDD for core modules (auth, bookings, credit, reviews). Playwright E2E with 15+ scenarios testing both happy and failure paths against the real Docker stack.

**UI:** spartan-ng (Angular shadcn/ui port) + Tailwind CSS, 8px grid, design tokens, shimmer loaders, full interaction states, visual audit checklist.

**Key additions:** state transition diagrams, full API contract table, role permission matrix, logging strategy, security audit checklist, README structure.

input.plan:
# JusticeOps — Production Implementation Plan

## 1. System Overview

**JusticeOps** is an offline-first legal services coordination platform for air-gapped/LAN-only environments. It manages legal casework, appointments, compliance tasks, and trust scoring across three roles: Client, Lawyer, and Administrator.

**Constraints:** No external APIs, Cloud KMS, CDN, public NTP, Redis, RabbitMQ, email, SMS, or push services. PostgreSQL is the sole persistence, queue, and audit engine.

**Tech Stack:**
- **Frontend:** Angular 19 (standalone components) + spartan-ng (Angular shadcn/ui port) + Tailwind CSS
- **Backend:** Fastify 5 (Node.js, TypeScript) with Hexagonal Architecture
- **Database:** PostgreSQL 16
- **Testing:** Vitest (unit/integration), Playwright (E2E), run_tests.sh unified entrypoint
- **Runtime:** Docker-first (`docker compose up --build` is the canonical start command)

---

## 2. Architecture Choice & Reasoning

### Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters) Architecture — Backend

```
backend/src/
├── domain/                    # Pure business logic — ZERO framework imports
│   ├── entities/              # User, Booking, Review, CreditScore, Job, etc.
│   ├── value-objects/         # Password, CreditRule, TimeSlot, IdempotencyKey, etc.
│   ├── services/              # Domain services (BookingService, CreditEngine, etc.)
│   ├── events/                # Domain event definitions
│   └── ports/                 # Interfaces (UserRepository, BookingRepository, etc.)
├── application/               # Use cases / application services — orchestrates domain
│   ├── auth/                  # LoginUseCase, LogoutUseCase
│   ├── bookings/              # CreateBookingUseCase, CancelBookingUseCase, etc.
│   ├── reviews/               # SubmitReviewUseCase, FileDisputeUseCase, etc.
│   ├── credit/                # ApplyCreditRuleUseCase, GetCreditHistoryUseCase
│   ├── jobs/                  # EnqueueJobUseCase, ProcessJobUseCase
│   ├── reports/               # GenerateReportUseCase, ExportReportUseCase
│   ├── notifications/         # CreateNotificationUseCase
│   └── webhooks/              # DispatchWebhookUseCase
├── infrastructure/            # Adapters — implements ports
│   ├── database/
│   │   ├── connection.ts      # Knex instance
│   │   ├── migrations/        # Numbered Knex migrations
│   │   ├── seeds/             # Seed data (auto-run in Docker)
│   │   └── repositories/      # KnexUserRepository, KnexBookingRepository, etc.
│   ├── encryption/            # AES-256-GCM adapter
│   ├── logging/               # Pino logger with PII masker
│   ├── auth/                  # JWT token adapter, session nonce manager
│   ├── rate-limiting/         # Token-bucket adapter (PostgreSQL-backed)
│   └── webhooks/              # HTTP webhook dispatcher
├── api/                       # Fastify HTTP layer — thin adapter
│   ├── plugins/               # Fastify plugins (auth, org-isolation, audit, time-sync)
│   ├── routes/                # Route definitions grouped by module
│   ├── schemas/               # Zod schemas for request/response validation
│   ├── middleware/             # preHandler hooks
│   └── server.ts              # Fastify app factory
├── workers/                   # Background job processing
│   ├── job-worker.ts          # FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED poller
│   ├── scheduler.ts           # node-cron (8AM reports w/ jitter, 2AM vacuum)
│   └── handlers/              # Job type handlers
└── main.ts                    # Entrypoint — wires everything together
```

**Why Hexagonal:**
- Domain logic (credit rules, booking validation, lockout) is testable without Fastify or PostgreSQL
- Infrastructure adapters are swappable (enables isolated unit tests with in-memory fakes)
- Clean dependency direction: domain ← application ← infrastructure/api

### Frontend Structure — Feature-Based Vertical Slices

```
frontend/src/app/
├── core/
│   ├── services/              # Auth, TimeSync, Notification, Idempotency
│   ├── interceptors/          # Auth header, error handling
│   ├── guards/                # Route-level role guards
│   └── models/                # Shared TypeScript interfaces
├── shared/
│   ├── components/            # Reusable: star-rating, permission-feedback, confirm-dialog,
│   │                          #   notification-bell, export-button, shimmer-loader, policy-banner
│   ├── directives/            # Permission directive, autofocus
│   └── pipes/                 # Date-from-server, credit-score-color
├── features/
│   ├── auth/                  # Login page
│   ├── client/                # Client workspace (dashboard, bookings, credit)
│   ├── lawyer/                # Lawyer workspace (dashboard, availability, requests)
│   ├── admin/                 # Admin workspace (dashboard, jobs, arbitration, users, orgs)
│   ├── reviews/               # Review form, list, dispute
│   ├── reports/               # Report viewer, export, subscriptions
│   └── notifications/         # Notification inbox
├── layout/
│   ├── shell/                 # App shell with sidebar + header + content area
│   ├── sidebar/               # Role-based navigation menu
│   └── header/                # Logo, user menu, notification bell
├── app.component.ts
├── app.routes.ts
└── app.config.ts
```

**UI Framework:** spartan-ng (Angular shadcn/ui) + Tailwind CSS with 4px/8px spacing scale, design tokens via Tailwind theme config, shimmer/spinner for all async states.

---

## 3. Domain Model

### Entities
```
User { id, orgId, username, passwordHash, role[client|lawyer|admin|super_admin],
       creditScore(0-100), isActive, isSessionExempt, failedLoginAttempts,
       lockedUntil, dailyCapacity(lawyers), createdAt, updatedAt }

Organization { id, name, settings(JSONB), createdAt, updatedAt }

Session { id, userId, sessionNonce, tokenJti, ipAddress, workstationId,
          expiresAt, createdAt }

Booking { id, clientId, lawyerId, orgId, type[consultation|milestone],
          status[pending|confirmed|completed|cancelled|no_show|declined|rescheduled],
          scheduledAt, deadlineAt, weight, completedAt,
          cancellationReasonEnc(BYTEA), idempotencyKey, createdAt, updatedAt }

Availability { id, lawyerId, dayOfWeek(0-6), startTime, endTime,
               slotDurationMin(default 60), isActive }

Review { id, bookingId, reviewerId, revieweeId,
         timeliness(1-5), professionalism(1-5), communication(1-5),
         comment(VARCHAR 1000), createdAt }

Dispute { id, reviewId, appellantId, reason, status[pending|under_review|resolved|dismissed],
          resolutionNotes, adminId, penaltyEscrowed(JSONB),
          filedAt, deadlineAt, resolvedAt }

CreditScoreHistory { id, userId, previousScore, changeAmount, newScore,
                     ruleCode, reason, isEscrowed, disputeId, createdAt }

Job { id, type, payload(JSONB), priority, status[queued|running|completed|failed|dead],
      attempts, maxAttempts(5), nextRetryAt, lastError, shardKey,
      idempotencyKey, lockedBy, createdAt, startedAt, completedAt }

Notification { id, userId, title, body, type, referenceId, isRead, createdAt }

ReportSubscription { id, userId, reportType, filters(JSONB), isActive, createdAt }

AuditLog { id(BIGSERIAL), userId, action, entityType, entityId,
           oldState(JSONB), newState(JSONB), ipAddress, createdAt }
           -- IMMUTABLE: REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE

IdempotencyRegistry { key(PK), userId, method, path, statusCode,
                      responseBody(JSONB), createdAt, expiresAt }

RateLimitBucket { id(PK), tokens, maxTokens, refillRate, lastRefillAt }

WebhookConfig { id, orgId, url, events(JSONB), secret, isActive, createdAt }

ConfigDictionary { id, orgId, category, key, value(JSONB) }

SystemConfig { key(PK), value(JSONB), updatedAt }
```

### Value Objects
```
Password — min 12 chars, ≥1 number, ≥1 symbol; validated at domain level
CreditRule — { ruleCode, changeAmount, description }; loaded from ConfigDictionary
TimeSlot — { date, startTime, endTime }; validated against availability
IdempotencyKey — UUID v4, 24h TTL
ServerTime — canonical time source; all business logic uses server clock
```

---

## 4. State Transitions

### Booking Lifecycle
```
[Created] → pending
  ├── Lawyer confirms → confirmed
  │     ├── Client/Lawyer completes → completed → [triggers review prompt]
  │     ├── No-show (10min past start, server time) → no_show → [credit -10]
  │     ├── Client cancels (>2h before) → cancelled
  │     └── Client cancels (<2h before) → cancelled → [credit -5]
  ├── Lawyer declines → declined
  ├── Client cancels → cancelled (no penalty if pending)
  └── Client reschedules → rescheduled → new booking created as pending
```

### Dispute Lifecycle
```
[Filed within 7 days of review] → pending → [penalties escrowed, score restored]
  ├── Admin reviews → under_review
  │     ├── Admin resolves (upholds appeal) → resolved → [escrowed penalties discarded]
  │     └── Admin dismisses (rejects appeal) → dismissed → [escrowed penalties re-applied]
```

### Credit Score Triggers
```
- Booking completed late:     -5  (rule: LATE_DELIVERY)
- No-show:                    -10 (rule: NO_SHOW)
- 5 consecutive on-time:      +2  (rule: STREAK_BONUS)
- Score < 20:                 Account throttled — bookings disabled (API returns 403)
- Dispute filed:              Penalty moved to escrow (score restored temporarily)
- Dispute resolved:           Escrowed penalty discarded
- Dispute dismissed:          Escrowed penalty re-applied
```

### Session Lifecycle
```
Login attempt:
  ├── locked_until > now() → 423 Locked (return remaining seconds)
  ├── Wrong password → increment failed_login_attempts
  │     └── attempts >= 5 → set locked_until = now() + 15min
  └── Correct password:
        ├── Delete existing session (unless is_session_exempt)
        ├── Create new session with fresh nonce
        └── Return JWT containing { userId, orgId, role, jti, nonce }

Every authenticated request:
  └── Validate JWT → lookup session by userId + nonce
        ├── Match → proceed
        └── No match → 401 "Session Revoked" (another workstation logged in)
```

---

## 5. Interface Contracts (API)

### Auth
| Method | Path | Request | Response | Auth | Errors |
|--------|------|---------|----------|------|--------|
| POST | /api/auth/login | `{ username, password }` | `{ token, user, menuPermissions, serverTime }` | None | 401 Invalid, 423 Locked |
| POST | /api/auth/logout | — | 204 | Bearer | 401 |
| GET | /api/auth/me | — | `{ user, creditScore, permissions }` | Bearer | 401 |
| GET | /api/time | — | `{ serverTime: ISO8601 }` | None | — |

### Users (Admin)
| Method | Path | Request | Response | Auth | Errors |
|--------|------|---------|----------|------|--------|
| GET | /api/users | `?orgId&role&page&limit` | `{ data[], total }` | Admin | 403 |
| POST | /api/users | `{ username, password, role, orgId, ... }` | `{ user }` | Admin | 422 Validation, 409 Duplicate |

## 6. Permission & Access Boundaries

### Role Matrix
| Resource | Client | Lawyer | Admin | SuperAdmin |
|----------|--------|--------|-------|------------|
| Own bookings | CRUD | Read+Confirm/Decline/Complete | Read all (org-scoped) | Read all (global) |
| Create booking | Yes (if credit≥20) | No | No | No |
| Own availability | No | CRUD | Read | Read |
| Reviews (own) | Create+Read | Create+Read | Read all | Read all |
| Disputes | File own | File own | Resolve all (org) | Resolve all (global) |
| Credit history | Own only | Own only | All (org-scoped) | All (global) |
| Users | Own profile | Own profile | CRUD (org-scoped) | CRUD (global) |
| Organizations | — | — | Read own | CRUD all |
| Reports/Dashboard | — | — | Full (org-scoped) | Full (global) |
| Jobs | — | — | Read (org-scoped) | Read (global) |
| Audit log | — | — | — | Read |
| Notifications | Own | Own | Own | Own |

### Enforcement Layers
1. **Route-level:** Fastify preHandler checks JWT role against route's allowed roles
2. **Resource-level:** Every query validates ownership (booking.clientId === userId) or org membership
3. **Database-level:** PostgreSQL RLS policies filter by `app.current_org_id` set per-transaction
4. **UI-level:** Menu items hidden + "Action Restricted: Requires {Role} Role" feedback on blocked actions

---

## 7. Failure Paths (Global)

Every API endpoint must handle these failure categories:

| Code | Condition | Response Body |
|------|-----------|---------------|
| 400 | Malformed JSON or missing required fields | `{ error: 'BAD_REQUEST', message, details[] }` |
| 401 | Missing/expired/invalid JWT, or session revoked | `{ error: 'UNAUTHORIZED', message: 'Session expired or revoked' }` |
| 403 | Insufficient role OR resource not owned OR credit < 20 | `{ error: 'FORBIDDEN', message: 'Action Restricted: ...' }` |
| 404 | Entity not found or not visible to user's org | `{ error: 'NOT_FOUND', message }` |
| 409 | Duplicate idempotency key (return cached), state conflict, slot taken | `{ error: 'CONFLICT', message }` |
| 422 | Zod validation failure | `{ error: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', message, details[] }` |
| 423 | Account locked out | `{ error: 'LOCKED', message, retryAfterSeconds }` |
| 429 | Rate limit exceeded | `{ error: 'RATE_LIMITED', message, retryAfterSeconds }` |
| 500 | Unhandled error | `{ error: 'INTERNAL_ERROR', message: 'An unexpected error occurred' }` (no stack trace) |

Global error handler: catches all thrown errors, maps to appropriate HTTP status, logs full stack trace via Pino (with PII masking), returns sanitized response to client.

---

## 8. Logging Strategy

**Library:** Pino (structured JSON logging)

**Log Levels:**
- `INFO` — request lifecycle, authentication events, booking state transitions, job state changes
- `WARN` — failed login attempts, rate limit hits, credit threshold warnings, approaching lockout
- `ERROR` — unhandled exceptions, job failures, webhook dispatch failures, encryption errors

**What gets logged (stdout — PII masked):**
- Request: method, path, status, duration, userId (not username), orgId
- Auth: login success/failure (masked username), lockout events, session revocations
- Bookings: state transitions with IDs (no client names)
- Jobs: enqueue, start, complete, fail with error type (no payload details)
- Credit: score changes with userId and rule code

**What NEVER gets logged to stdout:**
- Passwords, tokens, session nonces
- Full names, phone numbers, email addresses
- Booking notes, cancellation reasons (encrypted fields)
- Full request/response bodies

**Audit log (PostgreSQL — full unmasked JSONB):**
- Every mutating operation: OLD state, NEW state, userId, IP, timestamp
- Accessible only to super_admin via RLS
- Immutable (REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE on table)

**Implementation:** Custom Pino serializer in `infrastructure/logging/` that regex-matches and replaces PII patterns before output.

## 9. Docker Execution Plan

### docker-compose.yml Services
```yaml
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: justiceops
      POSTGRES_USER: justiceops
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-devpassword}
    ports: ["5432:5432"]
    volumes: [pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data]
    healthcheck:
      test: pg_isready -U justiceops
      interval: 5s

  backend:
    build: ./backend
    depends_on:
      db: { condition: service_healthy }
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://justiceops:${DB_PASSWORD:-devpassword}@db:5432/justiceops
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev-jwt-secret-change-in-production}
      ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${ENCRYPTION_KEY:-dev-encryption-key-32chars!!}
      NODE_ENV: production
    ports: ["3000:3000"]
    command: >
      sh -c "npm run migrate && npm run seed && node dist/main.js"

  frontend:
    build: ./frontend
    depends_on: [backend]
    ports: ["4200:80"]
    # Served via nginx in production container

  test-runner:
    build: ./backend
    depends_on:
      db: { condition: service_healthy }
    profiles: [test]
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://justiceops:${DB_PASSWORD:-devpassword}@db:5432/justiceops_test
      JWT_SECRET: test-jwt-secret
      ENCRYPTION_KEY: test-encryption-key-32chars!!!!
      NODE_ENV: test
    command: npm run test

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

### Backend Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./
COPY --from=builder /app/src/infrastructure/database/migrations ./migrations
COPY --from=builder /app/src/infrastructure/database/seeds ./seeds
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "dist/main.js"]
```

### Frontend Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npx ng build --configuration production

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=builder /app/dist/frontend/browser /usr/share/nginx/html
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
EXPOSE 80
```

### run_tests.sh
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e
echo "=== JusticeOps Test Suite ==="

echo "[1/4] Backend Unit Tests..."
cd backend && npm run test:unit

echo "[2/4] Backend Integration Tests..."
npm run test:integration

echo "[3/4] Frontend Unit Tests..."
cd ../frontend && npm run test

echo "[4/4] E2E Tests (Playwright)..."
cd ../e2e && npx playwright test

echo "=== ALL TESTS PASSED ==="
```

**Key Guarantee:** `docker compose up --build` starts a fully functional system with seeded data. No manual steps.

## 10. Module Breakdown — Vertical Slices

Each module below is a complete vertical slice. Implementation proceeds module-by-module. A module is NOT complete until it passes its defined tests, handles its failure paths, and works in Docker.

---

### MODULE 1: Foundation & Infrastructure
**Responsibilities:** Project scaffold, Docker, database connection, migrations, seeds, logging, encryption, config management.

**What it does:**
- Monorepo with `backend/`, `frontend/`, `e2e/`, `docker-compose.yml`, `run_tests.sh`
- All 8 database migrations creating every table, index, RLS policy, and constraint
- Knex connection pool with health check
- Pino logger with PII masking serializer
- AES-256-GCM encryption utility (encrypt/decrypt)
- Config loader (env vars with defaults)
- Seed data: 1 org, 1 super_admin, 1 admin, 2 lawyers, 2 clients, sample availability
- `system_config` table with `encryption_key_backup_confirmed = false`

**Inputs/Outputs:**
- Input: Environment variables (DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, ENCRYPTION_KEY)
- Output: Running Fastify server with health endpoint, connected DB, logger

**Failure Behavior:**
- Missing ENCRYPTION_KEY → server refuses to start with clear error
- DB connection failure → retry 3 times with backoff, then exit(1)
- Invalid migration → knex rollback, exit(1)

**Tests:**
- Unit: encryption encrypt/decrypt roundtrip, PII masker regex patterns, config validation
- Integration: migration up/down cycle, seed data verification, Knex connection health
- Docker: `docker compose up --build` starts cleanly, health endpoint returns 200

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] All migrations run without error
- [x] Seeds create valid test data
- [x] Logger masks PII in stdout
- [x] Encryption roundtrip works
- [x] Docker compose starts with zero manual steps
- [x] `GET /api/health` returns 200

---

### MODULE 2: Authentication & Session Management
**Responsibilities:** Login, logout, JWT issuance, session nonce tracking, lockout, password validation, single-session enforcement, admin exemption.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Login:** validate password → check lockout → issue JWT with nonce → revoke prior session → return token + profile + menu permissions + server time
2. **Logout:** delete session row → return 204
3. **Session validation:** every request extracts JWT → validates nonce against user_sessions → rejects if revoked
4. **Lockout:** 5 failures → 15min lock → auto-unlock after TTL
5. **Admin exempt:** `is_session_exempt` flag allows multiple concurrent sessions

**Inputs/Outputs:**
- POST /api/auth/login: `{ username, password }` → `{ token, user, menuPermissions, serverTime }`
- POST /api/auth/logout: Bearer token → 204
- GET /api/auth/me: Bearer token → `{ user, creditScore, permissions }`
- GET /api/time: → `{ serverTime: ISO8601 }`

**Failure Behavior:**
- Wrong password: 401, increment counter, log WARN
- 5th failure: 423 with `retryAfterSeconds`, log WARN
- Locked account: 423 even with correct password
- Expired JWT: 401
- Revoked session (another login): 401 "Session Revoked"
- Invalid password format on registration: 422 with rules

**Permission Boundaries:**
- /api/auth/login and /api/time are public
- /api/auth/me requires valid JWT + active session
- Password rules enforced in domain value object (not just API layer)

**Tests (TDD):**
- Unit (domain):
  - Password value object: valid/invalid patterns (12 chars, number, symbol)
  - Lockout logic: increment, threshold, TTL expiry
  - Session nonce generation uniqueness
- Unit (application):
  - LoginUseCase: success flow, wrong password, lockout trigger, lockout active, session revocation
  - LogoutUseCase: delete session
- Integration:
  - Full login flow against real DB
  - Session nonce validation middleware
  - Concurrent login revokes prior session
  - Admin exempt maintains multiple sessions
  - Lockout auto-expires after 15 minutes (mock time)
- API:
  - POST /api/auth/login — 200, 401, 423 responses
  - GET /api/auth/me — 200, 401
  - Rate of failed attempts → lockout sequence

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Password validation enforces all rules
- [x] JWT issued with jti + nonce
- [x] Session nonce checked on every authenticated request
- [x] New login revokes old session (unless exempt)
- [x] 5 failures trigger 15min lockout
- [x] Lockout returns 423 with countdown
- [x] Admin exempt flag works
- [x] All failure paths return correct HTTP codes
- [x] Docker verification passes

---

### MODULE 3: Authorization, Org Isolation & RBAC
**Responsibilities:** Role-based route guards, resource ownership validation, PostgreSQL RLS, org-scoped queries, permission metadata for UI menus.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Route authorization:** preHandler hook reads JWT role → checks against route's allowed roles → 403 if denied
2. **Resource ownership:** before returning/modifying any entity, verify userId or orgId matches
3. **RLS activation:** per-request `SET LOCAL app.current_org_id` in transaction
4. **Menu permissions:** login response includes `menuPermissions[]` describing which UI sections are accessible

**Failure Behavior:**
- Wrong role accessing route: 403 `{ error: 'FORBIDDEN', message: 'Action Restricted: Requires Admin Role' }`
- Accessing another user's resource: 404 (not 403, to prevent enumeration)
- Cross-org access attempt: RLS silently filters — returns empty or 404

**Tests (TDD):**
- Unit: authorize decorator logic, role hierarchy, permission matrix
- Integration:
  - Client cannot access /api/users (403)
  - Lawyer cannot access /api/reports/dashboard (403)
  - Client A cannot see Client B's bookings (404)
  - Org 1 admin cannot see Org 2 data (RLS)
  - SuperAdmin sees all orgs
- API:
  - Every endpoint tested with wrong role → 403
  - Cross-user resource access → 404

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Route-level role check on all protected endpoints
- [x] Resource ownership validated on all entity operations
- [x] RLS policies active and tested
- [x] Menu permissions returned on login
- [x] No data leakage across orgs or users

---

### MODULE 4: Booking Engine (Consultations & Milestones)
**Responsibilities:** Create, confirm, decline, cancel, reschedule, complete, no-show bookings. Advisory locks for consultations, capacity checks for milestones, idempotency, rate limiting.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Create consultation:** idempotency check → credit check (≥20) → rate limit → BEGIN → advisory lock on lawyer+timeslot → conflict check → INSERT → store idempotency response → COMMIT
2. **Create milestone:** idempotency check → credit check → rate limit → BEGIN → sum daily weights → capacity check → INSERT → COMMIT
3. **Cancel:** check if >2h before (server time) → if <2h, apply -5 credit → update status
4. **Reschedule:** cancel old + create new (idempotent pair)
5. **Complete:** update status → create notifications for both parties to review
6. **No-show:** check 10min past scheduled_at (server time) → apply -10 credit → update status

**Inputs/Outputs:** See API contracts in Section 5.

**Failure Behavior:**
- Credit < 20: 403 "Account Under Review"
- Rate limit exceeded: 429 with Retry-After
- Slot already booked: 409 "Time slot unavailable"
- Capacity exceeded: 409 "Lawyer's daily capacity is full"
- Duplicate idempotency key: return cached 200 response
- Invalid state transition (e.g., complete a cancelled booking): 409

**Tests (TDD):**
- Unit (domain):
  - Booking state machine: all valid/invalid transitions
  - TimeSlot overlap detection
  - Capacity weight calculation
  - Credit threshold check
  - Cancellation penalty window (2h, server time)
  - No-show grace period (10min, server time)
- Unit (application):
  - CreateBookingUseCase: happy path, credit block, capacity full, slot taken
  - CancelBookingUseCase: with/without penalty
  - CompleteBookingUseCase: triggers notification
- Integration:
  - Advisory lock prevents double-booking (two concurrent requests, one succeeds one gets 409)
  - Idempotency: same key returns cached response
  - Rate limiting: 21st request in a minute gets 429
  - Milestone capacity: sum correctly tracks daily weight
  - Full booking lifecycle: create → confirm → complete
- API:
  - All endpoint happy paths
  - All failure codes (403, 409, 422, 429)
  - Idempotency key reuse
  - State transition violations

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Consultations use advisory locks
- [x] Milestones use capacity-based logic
- [x] Idempotency with 24h key validity
- [x] Rate limiting (20/user, 200/org) from DB-persisted buckets
- [x] All state transitions enforced
- [x] Credit threshold blocks bookings
- [x] Policy time checks use server clock
- [x] All failure paths tested
- [x] Docker verification

### MODULE 7: Credit Score Engine
**Responsibilities:** Maintain 0-100 credit scores with configurable rules, explainable history, streak tracking, threshold gating.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Apply rule:** look up rule config → calculate change → clamp 0-100 → update user → log history with explanation
2. **Streak check:** on booking completion, check last 5 → if all on-time → apply +2
3. **Threshold gate:** if score < 20 → API rejects new bookings with 403
4. **Escrow integration:** dispute files → escrowed changes tracked in history

**Failure Behavior:**
- Score cannot go below 0 or above 100
- Rule code not found → use default config → log WARN

**Tests (TDD):**
- Unit (domain):
  - Credit clamping (boundaries: 0, 100, negative overflow, positive overflow)
  - Rule application with all standard rules
  - Streak detection (exactly 5, fewer than 5, broken streak)
  - Threshold check
  - Escrow/unesrow logic
- Integration:
  - Full credit lifecycle: no-show → score drops → dispute → escrow → resolve → score restored
  - Threshold blocks booking creation
  - History is complete and explainable
- API: GET /api/credit/:userId returns correct data for self and admin

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Rules: -5 late, -10 no-show, +2 streak
- [x] Configurable via config_dictionaries
- [x] History with explanation for every change
- [x] Threshold gating at API level
- [x] Escrow integration with disputes
- [x] 0-100 clamping

### MODULE 8: Job Queue & Scheduler
**Responsibilities:** Transactional outbox job queue (PostgreSQL only), FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED worker, exponential backoff retries, idempotent execution, scheduled tasks (8AM reports with jitter, 2AM vacuum).

**Required Flows:**
1. **Enqueue:** modules insert jobs within their business transactions (transactional outbox)
2. **Worker poll:** every 5s, SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED → claim → execute handler → update status
3. **Retry:** on failure, attempts++ → next_retry_at = now + 2^attempts seconds → max 5 attempts → dead
4. **8AM scheduler:** node-cron creates report-generation jobs for active subscriptions with random 0-15min jitter
5. **2AM vacuum:** purge idempotency_registry entries older than 24h
6. **Admin monitoring:** GET /api/jobs with filters for status, type, latency

**Failure Behavior:**
- Handler throws → job marked failed with error message, scheduled for retry
- 5th failure → job marked dead, admin notified
- Worker crashes → locked_by row is stale, picked up by another worker after timeout
- Duplicate idempotency key in job → skip execution (guard)

**Tests (TDD):**
- Unit:
  - Exponential backoff calculation
  - Jitter distribution (0-15min range)
  - Idempotency guard logic
  - Job state machine
- Integration:
  - Worker picks up queued jobs
  - Failed job retries with correct backoff
  - Dead job after 5 failures
  - Concurrent workers don't double-process (SKIP LOCKED)
  - Vacuum deletes expired idempotency keys
  - Report generation creates notification
- API: GET /api/jobs returns correct data with filters

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Pure PostgreSQL queue (no Redis/RabbitMQ)
- [x] FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED prevents double processing
- [x] Exponential backoff up to 5 retries
- [x] 8AM report gen with 15min jitter window
- [x] 2AM vacuum purges expired keys
- [x] Admin can monitor job state/latency/errors
- [x] Idempotent execution guards work

---

### MODULE 9: Reports & Export
**Responsibilities:** Dashboard metrics computation, CSV/Excel export, report subscriptions.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Dashboard:** compute availability%, fault rate, utilization, throughput, closed-loop efficiency from booking/job data
2. **Export:** generate CSV or XLSX file stream, return as download
3. **Subscriptions:** CRUD for scheduled report configs → drive 8AM job creation

**Metrics Definitions:**
- **Availability:** (total available slots - booked slots) / total available slots
- **Fault rate:** (no_shows + cancellations) / total bookings in period
- **Utilization:** completed bookings / total capacity in period
- **Throughput:** count of completed bookings in period
- **Closed-loop efficiency:** resolved alerts / total alerts in period

**Failure Behavior:**
- No data for date range: return zeroes, not error
- Invalid date range: 422
- Export of huge dataset: stream response, don't buffer in memory

**Tests:**
- Unit: metric calculation formulas with known data
- Integration: dashboard returns correct metrics from seeded data, export produces valid CSV/XLSX
- API: all endpoints, filter combinations, empty data handling

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] All 5 dashboard metrics computed correctly
- [x] Filters: org, date range, role
- [x] CSV and XLSX export work
- [x] Export streams (no memory bloat)
- [x] Subscriptions drive job creation
- [x] On-screen confirmation after export (frontend)

---

### MODULE 10: Notifications
**Responsibilities:** In-app notification CRUD, unread count, integration with job completion and booking events.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Create:** internal service called by booking completion, job completion, dispute updates
2. **List:** paginated, filterable by read/unread, includes total unread count
3. **Mark read:** single or mark-all

**Tests:**
- Integration: booking complete → notification created for both parties, job complete → subscription notification created
- API: list with unread filter, mark read, mark all read

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Notifications created on key events
- [x] Unread count in response
- [x] Mark read / mark all
- [x] Polling from frontend works

---

### MODULE 11: Webhooks & Integration
**Responsibilities:** Local webhook dispatch to on-prem endpoints, HMAC signing, retry, audit logging.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Dispatch:** on key events → query webhook_configs for org → POST with HMAC signature
2. **Retry:** 3 attempts with 5s backoff
3. **Audit:** log dispatch attempt/result to audit_log

**Failure Behavior:**
- Target unreachable: log error, retry, then mark failed
- Invalid URL: skip, log WARN

**Tests:**
- Integration: mock HTTP server receives webhook with correct HMAC
- Retry on failure, audit log entries created

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] HMAC-signed payloads
- [x] 3 retries with backoff
- [x] Audit trail for all dispatches
- [x] Configurable per org

---

### MODULE 12: Audit Trail & Security Hardening
**Responsibilities:** Immutable audit log with full OLD/NEW states, super_admin-only access, stdout log masking, encryption pre-save hooks, paper backup confirmation gate.

**Required Flows:**
1. **Audit capture:** Fastify onResponse hook captures all mutating operations with before/after state
2. **Paper backup gate:** on first start, all non-admin routes return 503 until admin confirms key backup
3. **Encryption hooks:** sensitive fields auto-encrypted before INSERT/UPDATE, auto-decrypted on SELECT

**Tests:**
- Integration:
  - Mutating operations create audit entries with correct old/new state
  - Audit log cannot be updated or deleted (PostgreSQL constraint)
  - Non-super_admin cannot query audit log
  - Paper backup gate blocks routes until confirmed
  - Encrypted fields roundtrip correctly
- Security:
  - stdout logs contain no PII (parse log output, verify masking)
  - JWT tokens not logged
  - Encryption key not logged

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Every mutation audited with OLD/NEW JSONB
- [x] Audit table is truly immutable
- [x] Super_admin-only access via RLS
- [x] PII masked in stdout
- [x] Sensitive fields encrypted at rest
- [x] Paper backup gate works
- [x] No secret leakage in logs

---

### MODULE 13: Frontend — Core, Layout & Auth
**Responsibilities:** Angular scaffold, spartan-ng/Tailwind setup, app shell (sidebar + header + content), role-based menu, auth service, time sync service, interceptors, guards, login page.

**What it does:**
- Angular 19 standalone with spartan-ng components + Tailwind CSS (8px grid, design tokens)
- App shell: grey sidebar (role-based nav), white content area, header with user menu + notification bell
- Login page: username/password form, password rules hint, lockout countdown, "session revoked" alert
- Auth service: login, logout, JWT storage, role observable
- Time sync service: GET /api/time on init + every 5min, expose `serverNow()`
- Auth interceptor: attach Bearer token
- Error interceptor: 401→login redirect, 403→permission feedback, 423→lockout display, 429→rate limit message
- Route guards: AuthGuard (logged in), RoleGuard (specific role)

**UI Requirements:**
- Shimmer skeletons on all data loading states
- 8px spacing grid via Tailwind config
- Design tokens for colors (CSS variables via Tailwind theme)
- All interactive elements: hover, active, focus, disabled states
- No layout shifts (reserved dimensions for dynamic content)
- Responsive layout (sidebar collapses on small screens)

**Tests:**
- Unit: auth service (token storage, role detection), time sync offset calculation, interceptor behavior (mock HTTP)
- E2E (Playwright):
  - Login happy path → redirects to role-appropriate dashboard
  - Login with wrong password → error message displayed
  - Login 5 times wrong → lockout message with countdown
  - Session revoked → redirected to login with message
  - Navigate to protected route without auth → redirect to login

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] App shell renders with role-based sidebar
- [x] Login works end-to-end with backend
- [x] Lockout display works
- [x] Session revocation detected and handled
- [x] Time sync service computes offset
- [x] Interceptors handle all error codes
- [x] Guards protect all routes
- [x] Shimmer loaders on async states
- [x] 8px grid, design tokens, no magic numbers
- [x] Docker: frontend container serves via nginx

---

### MODULE 14: Frontend — Client Workspace
**Responsibilities:** Client dashboard, booking creation (consultation + milestone), booking list, reschedule/cancel with policy messages, credit score view.

**UI Components:**
- Dashboard: upcoming bookings card, credit score gauge, policy banners
- Booking form: lawyer dropdown, available slot picker (for consultations), deadline + weight picker (milestones), policy confirmation dialog
- Booking list: table with status badges, action buttons (reschedule, cancel)
- Credit history: score display + timeline with explanations
- "Account Under Review" state: booking button disabled when credit < 20

**Policy Messages (always visible):**
- "No-show after 10 minutes past scheduled start counts as a violation"
- "Cancellations within 2 hours of the scheduled time incur a credit penalty"

**Tests:**
- Unit: booking form validation, policy message display logic, credit threshold UI logic
- E2E (Playwright):
  - Create consultation booking → appears in list as pending
  - Create milestone booking → appears in list
  - Cancel booking > 2h out → no penalty message
  - Cancel booking < 2h out → penalty warning dialog → confirm → penalty applied
  - Credit < 20 → "New Booking" disabled, "Account Under Review" visible
  - Policy messages visible on dashboard

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Full booking CRUD from client perspective
- [x] Policy messages always visible
- [x] Credit threshold disables bookings
- [x] Idempotency keys sent with requests
- [x] Server time used for policy calculations
- [x] Shimmer loaders, proper error states

---

### MODULE 15: Frontend — Lawyer Workspace
**Responsibilities:** Lawyer dashboard, availability management, booking request triage, workload capacity view.

**UI Components:**
- Dashboard: today's schedule, pending request count, daily capacity gauge
- Availability manager: weekly grid, add/edit/delete slots
- Booking requests: list with accept/decline buttons, client credit score visible
- Workload view: daily capacity bar showing milestone weight usage

**Tests:**
- Unit: availability form validation, capacity calculation display
- E2E (Playwright):
  - Set availability → slots appear in grid
  - Accept booking → status changes to confirmed
  - Decline booking → status changes to declined
  - View capacity gauge reflecting milestone weights

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Availability CRUD works
- [x] Accept/decline bookings works
- [x] Capacity gauge shows correct daily usage
- [x] Only own bookings visible

---

### MODULE 16: Frontend — Admin Workspace
**Responsibilities:** Operational dashboard with metrics, filters, job monitor, arbitration panel, user/org management, paper backup confirmation.

**UI Components:**
- Dashboard: 5 metric cards (availability, fault rate, utilization, throughput, closed-loop efficiency) + charts
- Filters: org dropdown, date range picker, role selector
- Job monitor: table with status/type/attempts/latency/error, color-coded badges
- Arbitration: dispute list (pending/resolved tabs), resolution form
- User management: table with CRUD, role selector, session exempt toggle
- Org management: table with CRUD (super_admin only)
- Paper backup modal: on first login if not confirmed

**Tests:**
- Unit: metric display formatting, filter state management
- E2E (Playwright):
  - Dashboard loads with metrics
  - Filter by org → metrics update
  - Job monitor shows jobs with correct states
  - Resolve dispute → status updates, score changes
  - Create user → appears in list
  - Paper backup modal appears on first admin login

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] All 5 metrics computed and displayed
- [x] Filters work across all views
- [x] Job monitor shows real job data
- [x] Arbitration flow works end-to-end
- [x] User/org CRUD works with proper roles
- [x] Paper backup gate works

---

### MODULE 17: Frontend — Reviews, Reports, Notifications
**Responsibilities:** Review submission, dispute filing, report viewer/export, subscription management, notification inbox + bell.

**UI Components:**
- Review form: 3 star-rating inputs + comment textarea (1000 char counter) — triggered after booking completion
- Review list: given/received tabs with averages
- Dispute form: reason textarea, "Appeal within 7 days" deadline display
- Report viewer: bar/line charts for dashboard metrics
- Export button: format selector (CSV/Excel), download trigger, "File saved to disk" confirmation toast
- Subscription manager: list with active toggle, create form
- Notification bell: header icon with unread badge, dropdown preview
- Notification inbox: full list with read/unread, type icons, timestamps

**Tests:**
- E2E (Playwright):
  - Complete booking → review prompt appears → submit review → appears in list
  - File dispute → appears in admin arbitration
  - Export report as CSV → file downloads
  - Create subscription → appears in list
  - Notification bell shows unread count → click → mark as read → count decreases

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] Two-way review flow works end-to-end
- [x] Dispute filing respects 7-day window
- [x] Export produces valid files with confirmation
- [x] Subscriptions create/manage correctly
- [x] Notification bell + inbox fully functional
- [x] All shimmer/loading states present

---

### MODULE 18: E2E Integration & Final Hardening
**Responsibilities:** Full system E2E tests via Playwright, Docker verification, security audit, visual audit, documentation.

**E2E Test Scenarios (Playwright — tests hit real backend):**
1. **Auth flow:** login → navigate → logout → protected route redirects
2. **Session revocation:** login on "workstation A" → login on "workstation B" → workstation A gets 401
3. **Lockout:** 5 wrong passwords → lockout message → wait (mock) → can login again
4. **Client booking lifecycle:** login as client → create consultation → see in list → lawyer confirms → complete → review prompt → submit review
5. **Milestone booking:** login as client → create milestone → verify capacity gauge updates on lawyer dashboard
6. **Cancellation penalty:** create booking → cancel within 2h → verify credit deducted
7. **No-show:** create + confirm booking → trigger no-show → verify credit deducted
8. **Credit threshold:** deplete credit below 20 → verify booking disabled
9. **Dispute flow:** submit review → file dispute → verify score escrowed → admin resolves → verify score restored
10. **Admin dashboard:** login as admin → verify all 5 metrics → apply filters → verify update
11. **Job monitor:** verify jobs visible with correct states
12. **Report export:** export CSV → verify file downloads
13. **Notifications:** trigger event → verify bell count → open inbox → mark read
14. **Permission feedback:** client tries admin URL → gets redirected; lawyer tries to create booking → gets 403 message
15. **Rate limiting:** rapid booking attempts → verify 429 handling in UI

**Visual Audit Checklist:**
- [ ] 8px grid consistent across all pages
- [ ] Design tokens used (no magic numbers in CSS)
- [ ] Distinct backgrounds for sidebar vs content vs header
- [ ] Typography hierarchy (H1, H2, body) consistent
- [ ] All interactive elements have hover/active/focus/disabled states
- [ ] Shimmer loaders on all async transitions
- [ ] No layout shifts (CLS)
- [ ] Icons consistent weight/style
- [ ] Responsive: sidebar collapses, tables scroll

**Security Audit:**
- [ ] No TODOs or mock code remaining
- [ ] No hardcoded secrets
- [ ] No absolute paths
- [ ] All API endpoints validate auth + ownership
- [ ] RLS active on all tenant-scoped tables
- [ ] Encrypted fields verified
- [ ] Audit log immutable
- [ ] PII masked in stdout
- [ ] No token/password in logs
- [ ] CORS configured for local only

**Docker Final Verification:**
```bash
docker compose down -v
docker compose up --build
# Wait for healthy
./run_tests.sh
```

**Completion Criteria:**
- [x] All 15+ E2E scenarios pass
- [x] Visual audit checklist passes
- [x] Security audit checklist passes
- [x] run_tests.sh passes in clean Docker environment
- [x] README.md complete
- [x] Zero TODOs, zero mock code, zero hardcoded values

---

## 11. Testing Strategy Summary

| Layer | Tool | What | Target |
|-------|------|------|--------|
| Domain Unit | Vitest | Entities, value objects, domain services | 95%+ of domain logic |
| Application Unit | Vitest | Use cases with mocked ports | All use cases, all branches |
| Infrastructure Integration | Vitest + Docker PG | Repositories, encryption, rate limiting | All adapters |
| API Integration | Vitest + Fastify inject | Route handlers, middleware chain, error responses | All endpoints, all error codes |
| Frontend Unit | Vitest/Karma | Services, interceptors, guards, component logic | Core services, shared components |
| E2E | Playwright | Full user flows against Docker stack | 15+ scenarios covering happy + failure paths |
| **Total target** | | | **≥90% meaningful behavioral coverage** |

**TDD preference:** Modules 2, 3, 4, 6, 7 (auth, RBAC, bookings, reviews, credit) are well-suited for TDD — define tests first. Modules 1, 5, 8-12 define expected tests before implementation but may implement test-alongside.

---

## 12. README.md Structure

```markdown
# JusticeOps — Offline Legal Services & Scheduling

## Overview
[1-paragraph system description]

## Prerequisites
- Docker & Docker Compose

## Quick Start
docker compose up --build
# Frontend: http://localhost:4200
# Backend API: http://localhost:3000
# Default credentials seeded (see below)

## Testing
./run_tests.sh

## Architecture
[Brief hexagonal architecture explanation with diagram]
[Module list with responsibilities]

## API Documentation
[Full endpoint table with methods, paths, request/response shapes, auth requirements]

## Seeded Test Accounts
| Username | Password | Role |
|----------|----------|------|
| admin | ... | super_admin |
| lawyer1 | ... | lawyer |
| client1 | ... | client |

## Environment Variables
[Table of all env vars with defaults and descriptions]

## Operational Notes
- Air-gapped: no external dependencies
- One active session per user (configurable admin exemption)
- Paper backup confirmation required on first start
- All times are server-authoritative
```

---

## 13. Implementation Order & Dependencies

```
Module 1: Foundation ──────────────────────────────┐
Module 2: Auth & Sessions ─────────────────────────┤
Module 3: Authorization & Org Isolation ───────────┤── Backend Core
Module 12: Audit & Security ───────────────────────┘
    │
    ├── Module 5: Availability ────────────────────┐
    ├── Module 4: Booking Engine ──────────────────┤── Business Logic
    ├── Module 7: Credit Score Engine ─────────────┤
    ├── Module 6: Reviews & Disputes ──────────────┘
    │
    ├── Module 8: Job Queue & Scheduler ───────────┐
    ├── Module 9: Reports & Export ────────────────┤── Platform Services
    ├── Module 10: Notifications ──────────────────┤
    └── Module 11: Webhooks ───────────────────────┘
    │
    ├── Module 13: Frontend Core & Auth ───────────┐
    ├── Module 14: Client Workspace ───────────────┤
    ├── Module 15: Lawyer Workspace ───────────────┤── Frontend
    ├── Module 16: Admin Workspace ────────────────┤
    └── Module 17: Reviews/Reports/Notifications ──┘
    │
    └── Module 18: E2E & Final Hardening ──────────── Final
```

Each module verified before proceeding to the next. Docker compose tested at every module boundary that affects services/startup.
